title: Product Designer (UI-focused)
description:
**About us**

  

Soundtrack is a B2B scale-up company providing music streaming services for businesses. We serve small customers like the café around the corner, and much bigger brands like Joe & the Juice, Toni & Guy and TAG Heuer. On the inside, we're a bunch of talented, motivated and humble designers, engineers and music experts. We believe in product-led growth, where the product is the primary driver of customer acquisition, conversion and expansion.

 **The Role**


We’re looking for a Product Designer (UI-focused) to join our Product Experience team at Soundtrack. You’ll play a key role in elevating the visual quality and usability of our core product, our web application and app used by customers every day.


This role is UI-heavy but grounded in strong product thinking. You’ll work end-to-end, from early discovery and concept exploration to delivering polished, production-ready design in close collaboration with engineers. We’re looking for someone who doesn’t just refine ideas, but actively shapes them.


You’ll collaborate closely with our Team Lead, Tech Lead, UX Designer and frontend developers, contributing to both larger product initiatives and continuous product improvements.


This is a permanent role based in Stockholm. We work hybrid, with at least two days per week from the office, with Mondays as our main team day.

**Responsibilities**

* Participate in product planning and discovery together with product and engineering
* Explore ideas and translate user needs and business goals into clear design solutions
* Create concepts, high-fidelity designs and interactive prototypes in Figma
* Deliver high-quality UI and ensure strong visual execution across the product
* Take designs all the way to implementation in close collaboration with frontend developers
* Contribute to and evolve our design system
* Drive visual consistency and raise the overall design quality in the team
* Work across both larger product initiatives and smaller, iterative improvements

**About You**


You are a strong visual product designer who enjoys owning the full journey from idea to shipped solution. You’re confident in your craft, comfortable in discussions, and enjoy being an active contributor in a collaborative product team.


You don’t just make things look good. You create thoughtful, structured and scalable solutions that improve the product experience. You’re proactive, self-driven and comfortable working closely with developers to ensure quality in implementation.


You value in-person collaboration and want to be part of shaping both the product and the team culture.

 **Requirements**

* 5+ years of experience as a Product Designer or UI Designer
* Experience designing apps and/or web applications, not only marketing or campaign sites
* Proven experience taking design from concept to shipped product
* Proficiency in Figma
* Comfortable working closely with engineers in cross-functional teams
* Strong communication skills and ability to drive your own work
* Fluent in English
* Ability to work from our Stockholm office at least two days per week

 **Employee Benefits**

* We offer a flexible workplace with the possibility to work remotely
* Generous pension plan
* Yearly budget for health, wellness and personal development
* Laptop and mobile phone of your choice with included cellular subscription
* Regular social activities such as team events, meet ups, after works and off-sites
* Daily breakfast served at the office

  

At Soundtrack, we believe that diversity fuels creativity and innovation. We're committed to building a team that reflects a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences. We welcome applications from individuals of all races, ethnicities, genders, ages, abilities, religions, sexual orientations, and identities.


We reserve the right to close this vacancy early if we identify a suitable candidate before the application deadline. To ensure consideration, we encourage you to submit your application as soon as possible.


Please note that we only accept applications submitted via our career page and do not accept applications by e-mail.

title: UX Writer / Content Designer
description:
**Your New Role**


This is a consultancy assignment covering parental leave, focusing on UX writing and content design for mobile and web experiences. The consultant will design user-focused content, including UI microcopy, instructional text, naming conventions, and information architecture. Responsibilities include managing content and translations in Figma and CMS systems to meet biweekly release schedules. Contributions may also involve Core features such as Account, Onboarding, and Voice. Collaboration with designers, researchers, product managers, engineers, and cross-functional stakeholders ensures a consistent and coherent user journey.

**Company Presentation**


The company is a leading global manufacturer of home and professional appliances, known for combining design, technology, and usability to improve everyday life. Operating across multiple markets worldwide, they create innovative, sustainable, and user-friendly products. The organization values creativity, collaboration, and continuous learning, promoting cross-functional teamwork and design thinking. High-quality experiences, accessibility, and consistent visual and content standards are central to how they deliver solutions across digital and physical touchpoints.

**Good to Know**


Scope of employment: Full-time, 100%  

Type of contract: Fixed-term consultant via JobBusters  

Working model: Hybrid, Stockholm-based  

Start date: 2026-03-16  

End date: 2026-09-06  

In your application: CV should clearly demonstrate relevant UX writing and content design experience, including Figma and CMS usage

**To succeed in this role, you will need:**

* 2+ years of experience writing content for web/mobile products
* Strong understanding of UX design principles and interaction patterns
* Fluency in English, written and spoken
* Experience with content management systems and Figma

**Personal qualities**


The consultant is proactive, organized, and comfortable in iterative design processes. They are able to communicate content rationale to stakeholders, champion accessibility and localization best practices, and contribute constructively to design critique.

**What we Offer**

* Secure consultancy engagement through JobBusters with professional support
* Access to occupational health services and wellness programs
* Long-term assignments and a dedicated Consultant Manager
* Opportunities to build experience, expand networks, and develop professional skills

**We look forward to receiving your application**


We review applications on an ongoing basis. As recruitment processes in the consulting industry can move quickly, the position may be filled before the advertised deadline — so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.


You do not need to submit a cover letter. Instead, please respond to the screening questions included in the application process.

title: UX Writer / Content Designer Stockholm ️
description:
Vill du forma användarupplevelsen i en global appmiljö? Nu söker vi en UX Writer som vill arbeta tvärfunktionellt i agila team och designa innehåll för smarta hem-produkter som robotdammsugare, luftrenare och luftkonditionering. Uppdraget passar dig som brinner för tydligt, tillgängligt och skalbart språk – och som vill bidra i ett datadrivet, iterativt produktutvecklingsflöde.

### **Om uppdraget**

Som UX Writer blir du en del av ett digitalt produktteam där du ansvarar för att skapa genomtänkta och sammanhängande innehållsupplevelser genom hela kundresan. Du arbetar nära design, produkt, research och utveckling, och samarbetar även med funktioner som Marketing, Post-Purchase, R&D och Brand för att säkerställa en konsekvent upplevelse. Uppdraget är ett föräldravikariat på tre månader med start i mars.

### **Dina arbetsuppgifter**

Du designar och utvecklar innehåll för appupplevelsen, inklusive informationsarkitektur, UI-mikrocopy, instruktionstexter, namngivning och andra delar av det end-to-end-flödet. Du hanterar och publicerar innehåll i Figma och CMS samt säkerställer att översättningar är klara inför tvåveckorsreleaser. Du bidrar till designsystemet genom att uppdatera global ordlista och ta fram riktlinjer och mönster för UX writing. Vid behov stöttar du även med översättningar, exempelvis till svenska, om du har språklig kompetens på modersmålsnivå.

### **Förväntade leveranser**

* Tydlig, användarcentrerad och tillgänglig mikrocopy för appens funktioner
* Uppdaterat och kvalitetssäkrat innehåll i Figma och CMS inför varje release
* Bidrag till designsystemet i form av riktlinjer, skrivmönster och uppdaterad terminologi
* Säkerställd och koordinerad lokalisering enligt tidsplan
* Konsistent tonalitet och kundresa i samarbete med relevanta intressenter

### **Din profil / Obligatoriska kompetenser**

* Minst 2 års erfarenhet av att skriva innehåll för webb- eller mobilprodukter
* God förståelse för UX-designprinciper och interaktionsmönster
* Flytande engelska i tal och skrift
* Erfarenhet av CMS-verktyg, exempelvis Phrase
* Erfarenhet av designverktyg som Figma
* Portfolio (online eller PDF) som visar bredd och kvalitet i ditt arbete

### **Meriterande färdigheter**

* Erfarenhet av lokalisering och tillgänglighetsanpassning
* Erfarenhet av att arbeta med globala produkter och flera språk
* Förmåga att bidra strategiskt till designsystem och skrivriktlinjer
* Modersmålsnivå i svenska eller annat språk

### **Personliga egenskaper**

Vi söker dig som förespråkar tillgängligt och kulturellt medvetet språk, som kan motivera dina designbeslut och som trivs i en iterativ Build-Measure-Learn-miljö. Du är självgående, strukturerad och van vid att hantera förändring och otydliga ramar. Du engagerar dig i konstruktiv designkritik och samarbetar prestigelöst med olika kompetenser.

**Plats**: Stockholm (Stadshagen), hybridarbete enligt gällande policy  

**Omfattning**: Heltid, 100 %  

**Period**: 2026-03-23 – 2026-06-30  

**Sista ansökningsdag**: 16 mars 2026


Sway Sourcing är en innovativ rekryteringspartner som specialiserar sig på att matcha rätt talang med rätt företag – snabbt och effektivt. Vårt huvudfokus ligger inom Ekonomi, Administration, HR, Marknad och IT, men vi har även den breda expertis och flexibilitet som krävs för att leverera skräddarsydda rekryteringslösningar inom alla branscher.


Trots att vi är en relativt ny aktör har vi redan byggt förtroende hos många av Sveriges största företag och arbetar både nationellt och internationellt. Med baser i Sverige och Spanien erbjuder vi en unik kombination av lokal expertis och global räckvidd. Vårt starka nätverk och djupa branschinsikter gör oss till en självklar partner för företag som vill ligga steget före i sin rekrytering.

title: Senior Tech Lead
description:
### **Välkommen till Shoppa!**

#### **Om oss**


Shoppa är ett etablerat SaaS-bolag som sedan 2003 levererar mjukvara till butikskedjor i flera europeiska länder, med starkast fäste i Norden. Vår plattform används dagligen i tusentals butiker och ger kontroll över kampanjer och skyltning, automatiserar flöden och sparar tid, vilket minskar kostnader och ökar försäljningen.


Med starkt och långsiktigt ägande (vi är sedan 2021 en del av Nordtech Group) och en tydlig marknadsposition har vi goda förutsättningar att investera vidare i produkt och teknik. Samtidigt är vi mitt i en teknisk förflyttning där arkitektur, kvalitet och arbetssätt utvecklas.


I takt med att bolaget växer stärker vi nu vår tekniska organisation med en Tech Lead. I rollen har du personalansvar för utvecklingsteamet och ansvar för struktur, prioritering och arbetssätt. Vi söker en senior teknisk ledare med både arkitekturdjup och hands-on-erfarenhet, som vill ta ett helhetsansvar för både teknik och team.

#### **Om rollen**


I rollen rapporterar du till VD och har ett tydligt operativt tekniskt ledarskap i nära samarbete med nuvarande CTO. Du tar ett samlat ansvar för vår SaaS-plattform och leder utvecklingsteamet i moderniseringen från on-prem till molnbaserad arkitektur. Samtidigt är du hands-on i utvecklingen och kliver in i de mer komplexa tekniska besluten och implementationerna. Du driver teknisk kvalitet genom tydliga principer, genomtänkta arkitekturbeslut och effektiv användning av moderna verktyg, inklusive AI-stöd i utvecklingsprocessen.

**I korthet innebär detta att du ansvarar för att:**

* sätta teknisk riktning och äga arkitekturfrågor
* säkerställa långsiktig kvalitet, struktur och hållbarhet i kodbasen
* leda och utveckla teamets tekniska kompetens
* driva modernisering och molntransformation på ett genomförbart sätt
* koppla tekniska beslut till affär och produktstrategi

#### **Vem är du?**


Du är en senior utvecklare med djup teknisk kompetens och erfarenhet av att ta arkitekturansvar i komplexa system.

**Du har sannolikt:**

* 8+ års erfarenhet av systemutveckling
* några års erfarenhet i en tekniskt ledande roll
* drivit större tekniska förändringar, exempelvis modernisering eller molnmigrering
* arbetat i miljöer där legacy och nyutveckling samexisterar


Vi tror att du har en tydlig backend-profil men förstår hela applikationsstacken och hur backend, frontend, UX och arkitektur tillsammans skapar värde i produkten. Du är trygg i att fatta tekniska beslut, sätta riktning och skapa struktur när komplexiteten ökar, och du har ett genuint intresse för att utveckla människor och bygga ett starkt engineering-team.

##### **Teknisk miljö**


Vi befinner oss i en övergång från on-prem till cloud och söker dig som har erfarenhet av liknande resor.

**Vi arbetar bland annat med:**

* C# och .NET (både .NET Framework 4.8 och modern .NET 6/8)
* React, Next.js och TypeScript
* Docker och Kubernetes (AKS)
* Azure DevOps och etablerade CI/CD-flöden
* REST, gRPC och viss SOAP i äldre delar
* OpenID Connect / OAuth2
* Azure Database for MySQL


Du behöver inte vara specialist på allt, men du har god förståelse för arkitektur i distribuerade system och erfarenhet av att arbeta i liknande miljöer.

#### **Vi erbjuder**

* En innovativ och avslappnad arbetsmiljö med stora möjligheter till professionell utveckling.
* Konkurrenskraftig lön och fina förmåner.
* Möjligheten att arbeta med ett dedikerat och kunnigt team som även tycker om att hitta på sociala aktiviteter tillsammans!
* Hybridarbete med möjlighet att arbeta från vårt kontor i Enköping.

#### **Ansökan**


Låter detta som ditt nästa steg i karriären? Tveka inte att skicka in din ansökan, vi ser fram emot att höra från dig!

*I denna rekryteringsprocess tillämpar vi psykometriska tester från* *Alva Labs**. Anledningen till det är för att säkerställa att vi ger dig, och andra kandidater, en rättvis chans att visa sin lämplighet för rollen :)*

title: Growth Marketing Manager – Lifecycle
description:
**About Us**
 Soundtrack is a B2B scale-up company providing music streaming services for businesses. We serve small customers like the café around the corner, and much bigger brands like Joe & the Juice, Toni & Guy and TAG Heuer. On the inside, we're a bunch of talented, motivated and humble designers, engineers and music experts. We believe in product-led growth, where the product is the primary driver of customer acquisition, conversion and expansion.
   

  

**The Role**
 As our Growth Marketing Manager, Lifecycle, you’ll turn our customer communications into a growth engine. We’re looking for a strategic idea generator, someone who can analyze the journey, spot opportunities, and turn those insights into executed, refined campaigns.
   

  

 You are a hands-on problem solver with a "good eye" for content and a curiosity for how new technology can supercharge the area. While you’ve likely worked with CRM tools before, we care more about your ability to think in systems and learn new platforms.
   

  

 You’ll Drive Tangible Business Outcomes Across The Funnel And Act As a Central Point For Teams That Communicate With Our Customers (Sales, Product, And Customer Success). High-priority Projects Will Include
   

  

* Redefining our comms during our Welcome and Onboarding experience.
* Building smart re-engagement and win-back messaging.
* Sales-assisted introductions.


 You’ll be the first in this role at Soundtrack, meaning you’ll be handling everything from strategy and copywriting to campaign setup. However, you’ll work within a world-class Growth and Marketing team of developers, designers, and analysts, so expert support is always within reach. Our lifecycle efforts are in a "startup" phase; you should be comfortable wearing different hats to move fast.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Drive growth: Impact key business metrics by solving customer friction points through communication.
* Coordinate: Act as the central lead for customer communication strategy across the company.
* Execute & Iterate: Write, deploy, learn and A/B test.
* Lead with product: Implement automated onboarding flows that guide trial users to "a-ha!" moments and falling in love with Soundtrack.
* Collaborate: Partner with Analytics and Insights teams to track performance (activation, conversion, retention) and turn data into better campaigns.
* Innovate: Continuously evaluate our stack and workflows.


**About You**
 Just like us, you love music. You're driven and entrepreneurial, but you know how to be a team player too. Regardless of roles, we're always looking to work with people who can adapt to constant change, prioritise what's important, stay humble, open, curious and with a passion for details.
   

  

**Requirements**
* 5+ years of experience in Growth or Lifecycle Marketing roles.
* Track record: You’ve used communication as a primary tool to drive measurable improvements across a customer journey.
* Insights-driven: Proficient in using everything from user research to A/B test results to inform your next move.
* "Good Eye": You have high standards for creative and content. You know what looks good and what drives action.
* Fluent English & writing skills: You draft clear, persuasive and "human" copy. You don’t need to be a professional copywriter, but you know how to write messages that people actually engage with and want to read.
* Technical skillset: Experience with CRM/Automation tools (like Intercom, HubSpot, Braze, Customer.io) is a strong plus, but we’re looking for a profile with a general ability to learn new systems and adapt new technology quickly.
* AI acumen: You’re already using AI to work smarter and faster.


**Employee Benefits**
* We offer a flexible workplace with the possibility to work remotely
* Generous pension plan
* Yearly budget for health, wellness and personal development
* Laptop and mobile phone of your choice with included cellular subscription
* Regular social activities such as team events, meet ups, after works and off-sites
* Daily breakfast served at the office


 At Soundtrack, we believe that diversity fuels creativity and innovation. We're committed to building a team that reflects a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences. We welcome applications from individuals of all races, ethnicities, genders, ages, abilities, religions, sexual orientations, and identities.
   

  

 We reserve the right to close this vacancy early if we identify a suitable candidate before the application deadline. To ensure consideration, we encourage you to submit your application as soon as possible.
   

  

 Please note that we only accept applications submitted via our career page and do not accept applications by e-mail.

title: Product Designer
description:
Anyfin is on a mission to change millions of financial lives for the better. Since our start over seven years ago, we’ve been doing this one customer at a time — with user experience and design at the core of our offering. But we aim higher; new customers, new products, and new design challenges. That’s why we are currently looking for a
 **Product Designer** 
 to join our Design team.
   

  

**What You Will Be Doing**
 Your focus will be on creating intuitive, user-centric solutions that truly help people understand and manage their finances — every single day. This means collaborating closely with Product, Engineering, Marketing, and Customer Service teams across all our markets to ensure we’re building thoughtful, effective, and accessible product experiences.
   

  

 A big part of your work will involve driving design efforts from exploration to launch and beyond. You’ll rapidly prototype, iterate, and explore multiple directions to uncover the best possible solutions. You will also contribute to and evolve our shared Design System, ensuring visual consistency and design quality across the entire product ecosystem.
   

  

 We adopt a hypothesis-driven approach, grounding our work in user feedback, data, and structured thinking. At Anyfin, the products you design genuinely matter — helping people take control of their personal finances.
   

  

**Who You Are**
 We’re looking for someone with a strong passion for visual excellence, systems thinking, and user experience — someone who wants to stay hands-on with the craft while collaborating across teams. You inspire those around you and help raise the overall design quality across the organization. And yes, here come the bullet points (they do serve a purpose):
   

  

* A proven track record as a product designer in a fast-paced environment, ideally designing B2C products that make a real difference to users.
* Strong UI craft and a solid understanding of UX design patterns.
* A portfolio that demonstrates clear, structured thinking and a user-centered design approach.
* Experience driving design work end-to-end: exploration, prototyping, iteration, delivery, and continuous improvement.
* The ability to quickly explore multiple design directions and use feedback to guide decision-making.
* Strong communication skills — you present your ideas clearly, manage stakeholders effectively, and tie design decisions to measurable outcomes and business goals.
* Experience collaborating closely with Product, Engineering, and Marketing teams.
* Openness to feedback and a desire to grow both personally and in how design impacts teams and the broader organization.
* Bonus points if you’ve contributed to or scaled design systems across teams.


**What We Offer**
 We offer you the chance to be part of something truly meaningful. You’ll work alongside colleagues who are ridiculously passionate about users and the experience we deliver to them. We promise you’ll be challenged, supported, and given the opportunity to work on things that actually matter.
   

  

 Then there’s the competitive compensation package, our centrally located and cozy office, extra time off on your birthday, and a bunch of other benefits. But we’ll get to all that in due time. For now, begin your journey with us by clicking
 **"Apply"** 
 below.
   

  

 We cannot wait to meet you!
   

  

 Anyfin is a fintech on a mission to simplify loan repayments and improve the financial well-being of millions. We help consumers reduce the costs of high-interest loans, and take control of their repayment plan.

title: Product Designer (UI-focused)
description:
**About Us**
 Soundtrack is a B2B scale-up company providing music streaming services for businesses. We serve small customers like the café around the corner, and much bigger brands like Joe & the Juice, Toni & Guy and TAG Heuer. On the inside, we're a bunch of talented, motivated and humble designers, engineers and music experts. We believe in product-led growth, where the product is the primary driver of customer acquisition, conversion and expansion.
   

  

**The Role**
 We’re looking for a Product Designer (UI-focused) to join our Product Experience team at Soundtrack. You’ll play a key role in elevating the visual quality and usability of our core product, our web application and app used by customers every day.
   

  

 This role is UI-heavy but grounded in strong product thinking. You’ll work end-to-end, from early discovery and concept exploration to delivering polished, production-ready design in close collaboration with engineers. We’re looking for someone who doesn’t just refine ideas, but actively shapes them.
   

  

 You’ll collaborate closely with our Team Lead, Tech Lead, UX Designer and frontend developers, contributing to both larger product initiatives and continuous product improvements.
   

  

 This is a permanent role based in Stockholm. We work hybrid, with at least two days per week from the office, with Mondays as our main team day.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Participate in product planning and discovery together with product and engineering
* Explore ideas and translate user needs and business goals into clear design solutions
* Create concepts, high-fidelity designs and interactive prototypes in Figma
* Deliver high-quality UI and ensure strong visual execution across the product
* Take designs all the way to implementation in close collaboration with frontend developers
* Contribute to and evolve our design system
* Drive visual consistency and raise the overall design quality in the team
* Work across both larger product initiatives and smaller, iterative improvements


**About You**
 You are a strong visual product designer who enjoys owning the full journey from idea to shipped solution. You’re confident in your craft, comfortable in discussions, and enjoy being an active contributor in a collaborative product team.
   

  

 You don’t just make things look good. You create thoughtful, structured and scalable solutions that improve the product experience. You’re proactive, self-driven and comfortable working closely with developers to ensure quality in implementation.
   

  

 You value in-person collaboration and want to be part of shaping both the product and the team culture.
   

  

**Requirements**
* 5+ years of experience as a Product Designer or UI Designer
* Experience designing apps and/or web applications, not only marketing or campaign sites
* Proven experience taking design from concept to shipped product
* Proficiency in Figma
* Comfortable working closely with engineers in cross-functional teams
* Strong communication skills and ability to drive your own work
* Fluent in English
* Ability to work from our Stockholm office at least two days per week


**Employee Benefits**
* We offer a flexible workplace with the possibility to work remotely
* Generous pension plan
* Yearly budget for health, wellness and personal development
* Laptop and mobile phone of your choice with included cellular subscription
* Regular social activities such as team events, meet ups, after works and off-sites
* Daily breakfast served at the office


 At Soundtrack, we believe that diversity fuels creativity and innovation. We're committed to building a team that reflects a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences. We welcome applications from individuals of all races, ethnicities, genders, ages, abilities, religions, sexual orientations, and identities.
   

  

 We reserve the right to close this vacancy early if we identify a suitable candidate before the application deadline. To ensure consideration, we encourage you to submit your application as soon as possible.
   

  

 Please note that we only accept applications submitted via our career page and do not accept applications by e-mail.

title: Web Producer
description:
****About Plus1****
 Plus1 is on a mission to end "ostrich economics." We help people lift their heads, take control, and get their finances back in the plus. We aren't a traditional bank – we are a financial partner. We simplify the complex, negotiate better terms, and consolidate loans to give our customers peace of mind and more money left at the end of the month.
   

  

 As a part of the 0TO9 ecosystem, Plus1 combines high-tech financial solutions with a human touch. We’ve already helped our customers save over 2 billion SEK, and as we scale into new markets, we need someone who can help us make the complex feel simple through world-class digital experiences.
   

  

****The Role****
 We are looking for a Web Producer to join our growing marketing team. This is a role for someone with a strong eye for design and a deep understanding of user experience. You will work closely with our team of three, taking full ownership of our site’s UX, design, and flow.
   

  

 You’ll be the bridge between brand and tech, ensuring that every visitor to our site has a world-class experience. We are scaling fast into new markets, so you need to be comfortable working internationally and moving at startup speed.
   

  

****What you will do****
* Own and develop the website experience with a focus on UX and visual impact
* You will work in Webflow, a no-code web platform.
* Work with a Brand Design Toolbox in Figma and Canva.
* Manage and update content across multiple markets to support our international expansion.
* Continuously optimize the site for conversion and user engagement.
* Collaborate daily with the marketing team to align web updates with global campaigns.
* Stay ahead of the curve by implementing AI-driven tools and innovative web solutions.


****Who you are****
 We are in a fast-moving scaleup phase that requires you to be a brave self-starter who isn't afraid to challenge the status quo. You are AI-driven, always looking for new ways to work smarter, and you keep a sharp eye on the latest digital trends.
   

  

* Minimum of 3-4 years of experience in a similar role
* Proficient in Webflow (or similar), Figma and Canva with a strong eye for design
* Experienced in startup or scale-up environments where you’ve handled international launches
* Independent and proactive, you bring proposals to the table before we even ask
* A positive "people person" who thrives in a high-ceiling team culture
* Fluent in both Swedish and English


****What we offer****
 You’ll be joining us at the most exciting time possible. As a key player in a fast-growing fintech, your energy and ideas won't just be heard, they’ll actually change how we work. We believe in giving talented people the space to grow, meaning there are massive opportunities to take on more responsibility as Plus1 continues to expand into new markets.
   

  

 You will be based in our stunning headquarters in the heart of Stockholm, proudly nominated as Sweden’s Most Beautiful Office 2025. Most importantly, you’ll join a team that truly loves what they do, values bold innovation, and keeps the ceiling high.

title: Entry Level Product Manager | Sweden | Remote
description:
Grafana Labs is a remote-first, open-source powerhouse. There are more than 20M users of Grafana, the open source visualization tool, around the globe, monitoring everything from beehives to climate change in the Alps. The instantly recognizable dashboards have been spotted everywhere from a NASA launch and Minecraft HQ to Wimbledon and the Tour de France. Grafana Labs also helps more than 3,000 companies -- including Bloomberg, JPMorgan Chase, and eBay -- manage their observability strategies with the Grafana LGTM Stack, which can be run fully managed with Grafana Cloud or self-managed with the Grafana Enterprise Stack, both featuring scalable metrics (Grafana Mimir), logs (Grafana Loki), and traces (Grafana Tempo).
   

  

 We’re scaling fast and staying true to what makes us different: an open-source legacy, a global collaborative culture, and a passion for meaningful work. Our team thrives in an innovation-driven environment where transparency, autonomy, and trust fuel everything we do.
   

  

 You may not meet every requirement, and that’s okay. If this role excites you, we’d love you to raise your hand for what could be a truly career-defining opportunity.
   

  

 This is a fully remote position and we're considering candidates in Sweden.
   

  

**About Product Management**
 As a Product Manager at Grafana Labs, you'll develop a vision and strategy for your product domain. You will own your product’s roadmap, collateral, and market research, and you will collaborate with Grafana leadership on fine-tuning your product’s goals. You will be the primary advocate and expert in your area, with a strong ability to communicate needs, functionality, and details effectively both to external customers and internal stakeholders. The teams and people you will be interacting with will be 100% remote and globally distributed, so strong and proactive communication is essential.
   

  

 Product Managers are the bridge that connect Engineering, Design, and UX to GTM. You are a facilitator who ensures the free flow of information across these teams and are expected to be technically proficient when representing our products to our customers.
   

  

**The Opportunity**
 As a Product Manager for AI, you’ll be involved with everything related to our AI offerings, including helping drive the roadmap of our Grafana Assistant ecosystem. You’ll get to the bottom of what customers want and challenge us on what we should build next.
   

  

 You’ll work across Grafana’s R&D and GTM orgs to deliver AI products and marketing messages. You will develop a deep understanding of how to apply AI to address customer problems in observability. And you’ll help shape Grafana Labs AI vision.
   

  

**What You’ll Be Doing**
* Partnering with our senior AI PM to define and own AI features end-to-end
* Working closely with our product marketing and sales teams to shape our AI messaging and support large commercial deals
* Understanding competitors, working with partners and staying on top of the broader AI market
* Being a hands-on builder: prototyping, building, and shipping AI features end-to-end with our engineering team
* Partnering with our data function to analyze large amounts at scale about our product, usage patterns and commercials
* Empathizing with customer problems in order to collect user needs, requirements, and pain points
* Curating and properly representing user feedback to Engineering, while providing evidence to support tradeoffs and priorities
* Using your deep product expertise to make decisions about our go-to-market strategies, pricing, packaging, licensing, and tiering


**What Makes You a Great Fit**
* Excellent written and verbal communication
* Strong product sense and curiosity about AI, including demonstrated evidence such as AI projects, blog posts and community contributions
* 2 years of product experience or equivalent experience in a related role with ownership of a feature or project
* Comfortable with basic data analysis (SQL, spreadsheets) and able to interpret experiment results
* Familiarity with large language models (LLMs), prompt engineering, or experience using open-source models


**Bonus Points**
* Experience using or building observability products and/or developer tools, either internally as an SRE or for external customer


**In Sweden, the base compensation range for this role is SEK 686,964 - SEK 824,356. Actual compensation may vary based on level, experience, and skillset as assessed throughout the interview process. All of our roles include Restricted Stock Units (RSUs), giving every team member ownership in Grafana Labs' success. We believe in shared outcomes—RSUs help us stay aligned and invested as we scale globally.**
* Compensation ranges are country specific. If you are applying for this role from a different location than listed above, your recruiter will discuss your specific market’s defined pay range & benefits at the beginning of the process.


**Why You’ll Thrive At Grafana Labs**
* 100% Remote, Global Culture - As a remote-only company, we bring together talent from around the world, united by a culture of collaboration and shared purpose.
* Scaling Organization – Tackle meaningful work in a high-growth, ever-evolving environment.
* Transparent Communication – Expect open decision-making and regular company-wide updates.
* Innovation-Driven – Autonomy and support to ship great work and try new things.
* Open Source Roots – Built on community-driven values that shape how we work.
* Empowered Teams – High trust, low ego culture that values outcomes over optics.
* Career Growth Pathways – Defined opportunities to grow and develop your career.
* Approachable Leadership – Transparent execs who are involved, visible, and human.
* Passionate People – Join a team of smart, supportive folks who care deeply about what they do.
* In-Person onboarding - We want you to thrive from day 1 with your fellow new ‘Grafanistas’ to learn all about what we do and how we do it.
* Balance is Key - We operate a global annual leave policy of 30 days per annum. 3 days of your annual leave entitlement are reserved for Grafana Shutdown Days to allow the team to really disconnect. \*We will comply with local legislation where applicable.


**Equal Opportunity Employer:** 
 We will recruit, train, compensate and promote regardless of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, disability, age, veteran status, and all the other fascinating characteristics that make us different and unique. We believe that equality and diversity builds a strong organization and we’re working hard to make sure that’s the foundation of our organization as we grow.
   

  

*Grafana Labs may utilize AI tools in its recruitment process to assist in matching information provided in CVs to job postings. The recruitment team will continue to review inbound CVs manually to identify alignment with current openings.*
*For information about how your personal data is used once you’ve applied to a job, check out our privacy policy.*

title: Marketing Coordinator
description:
As we are currently growing quickly and have an ambitious plan for the future, we are now seeking a talented and creative Marketing Coordinator to join our dynamic marketing and Nordic team. This team consists of experts in SEO, performance marketing, social media, UX, and analytics.
   

  

 The ideal candidate will be passionate about storytelling and creating engaging content across various platforms. The role includes developing social media content and acting as a ‘spider-in-the-web’ between local markets and the central marketing team (CMT), coordinating marketing activities across different teams. The responsibilities will also include defining and driving new marketing initiatives such as organizing and optimizing global webinars and supporting the rollouts of campaigns.
   

  

 The location for the role is at STS & Educatius office in central Stockholm.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities:**
* Develop and edit content for our website, blog, email campaigns, and other marketing materials.
* Coordinate various marketing tasks and support rollouts of activities to the Nordic markets.
* Engage with students globally and secure assets to social media and other channels
* Develop, post and manage social media channels across the STS & Educatius
* Produce visually appealing multimedia content, including videos, infographics and photos.
* Work in an international environment with multiple markets across the world.


**Requirements:**
* Proven experience as a content creator, marketing coordinator, or similar role.
* Basic graphic design skills and familiarity with design tools (e.g., Canva etc).
* Ability to work independently and manage multiple projects simultaneously.
* Excellent communication and collaboration skills.
* A creative mindset with a keen eye for detail.
* Great coordination and delivery-on-time skills
* Mandatory to have high level written & verbally of English and Swedish/Danish or Finnish


**Preferred Qualifications:**
* Experience in the education sector or with international student programs.
* Familiarity with video editing software, email marketing tools, social media scheduling and monitoring tools, Meta Ads Managers and TikTok Ads Manager
* Fluent in Danish, Norwegian, Swedish or Finnish


**What We Offer:**
* A collaborative and supportive work environment.
* Opportunities for professional growth and development.
* The chance to make a meaningful impact on students' lives.
* Competitive salary and benefits package.
* Great location in central Stockholm


**How To Apply**
 Are you ready to start your journey with STS and join our wonderful team? Please make sure to apply as soon as possible, we look forward to meeting you! Make sure to apply by 15th of March 2026, but please be aware that if we meet the perfect candidate sooner the position might be filled before that date.

title: Senior Product Design Lead
description:
At Anyfin, we believe finance should work in favor of the consumer – not the other way around. That’s why we build smart, transparent and customer-first solutions that make everyday finances easier and fairer.
   

  

 But we aim higher: more customers, products, markets… all of which need a well-designed, flawless experience. That’s why we’re currently looking for a
 **Senior Product Design Lead** 
 to join us and elevate our product experience across the board.
   

  

**What You Will Be Doing**
 As a Senior Product Design Lead at Anyfin, you will drive the vision, quality, and execution of the design experience across multiple product areas. You connect design quality to strategy and measurable business results.
   

  

 You will lead the Product Design team while working hands-on the majority of your time. When we say lead, we mean
 *by example* 
 : you maintain a high bar for visual and interaction design and bring clarity to complex problem spaces. You’re a true player/coach — someone who can zoom into the finer details of the craft while coaching others to raise our collective output.
   

  

 Collaboration is central to the role. You don’t just work closely with Product, Engineering, and Marketing—you define and evolve the ways of working across discovery, delivery, and validation, ensuring what we ship is beautiful, intuitive, strategically aligned, and measurably impactful. You will guide teams through discovery, delivery, and validation and help break down complex financial concepts into clear, elegant, user-friendly experiences.
   

  

 Your work will have a direct impact on how customers interact with their finances, and your contributions will play a big part in our mission of making financial well-being happen.
   

  

**Who You Are**
 We’re looking for someone who thrives at the intersection of leadership, craft, and strategic thinking — someone who elevates the team while pushing the quality of the product experience forward. Here’s what we’re looking for:
   

  

* You have deep experience leading product design in fast-paced environments and are comfortable guiding multiple product areas at once. Fintech or financial services experience is a bonus.
* You bring a deep understanding of UX design patterns and usability principles, and you set a consistently high visual and interaction bar.
* You excel at translating complex problems into simple, elegant, user-centric solutions.
* You have strong native mobile design expertise, from navigation patterns to platform conventions.
* You lead cross‑functional collaboration with Product, Engineering, and Marketing, aligning stakeholders and decisions across multiple product areas.
* You’re comfortable defining and running product discovery and validation activities and tying design decisions directly to business goals and measurable outcomes.
* You design within regulatory and compliance constraints (e.g., KYC/AML, disclosures) without sacrificing clarity or user experience.
* You’re comfortable defining and running product discovery and validation activities, and you consistently link design decisions to business goals and measurable outcomes.
* You have experience designing within regulatory or compliance constraints without sacrificing user experience.
* You have experience owning or scaling design systems — ensuring accessibility, consistency, and efficiency across teams.


**What We Offer**
 We offer you the chance to be part of something truly meaningful. You’ll work alongside colleagues in Product, Engineering, Marketing, Credit, Operations and more — giving you a broad playground to influence and shape. We promise you’ll be challenged, supported, and given work that truly matters.
   

  

 Beyond the mission, we offer a competitive compensation package, a centrally located and cozy office, extra time off on your birthday, and plenty of other benefits along the way.
   

  

 If you’re ready to help build the future of user-centric financial products, click “Apply” below.
   

  

 We can’t wait to meet you!
   

  

 Anyfin is a fintech on a mission to simplify loan repayments and improve the financial well-being of millions. We help consumers reduce the costs of high-interest loans, and take control of their repayment plan.

title: Rider Experience Manager
description:
**YOUR MISSION AT VOI** 




 As
 **Rider Experience Manager** 
 you will report to the Head of Rider Experience and work continuously to drive improvements to the service design of Voi’s rider experience. In this role, you will build tools, processes and guidelines to identify and understand performance gaps, and support local market and R&D teams in mitigating business risk and taking concrete actions to drive product optimisation, market growth and P&L optimisation. This will include:
 



  




* **Tools & Processes** 
 : Developing and driving continuous improvement of tools, metrics, dashboards and processes to accurately monitor and represent the end-to-end rider experience across markets and compliance with local permit requirements
 **.**
* **Analysis & Insights** 
 : Analysing data, synthesising insights and building business cases to understand performance gaps, risks, and the impact and prioritisation of improvement opportunities
 **.**
* **Business optimisation enablement** 
 : Building frameworks, benchmarks and guidelines, and presenting actionable insights to advise and enable regional directors, country managers and local teams to understand performance gaps and take concrete actions to support market growth and P&L optimisation
 **.**
* **Product optimisation enablement:** 
 Providing R&D teams with synthesised insights, hypotheses and recommendations based on cross-market patterns, rider pain points and performance data to support ideation and prioritisation of development and new features in the product roadmap
 **.**
* **New product rollout** 
 : Supporting new product rollout, implementation and adaptation across markets
 **.**
* **Tender support:** 
 Contributing service design, rider experience and product optimisation expertise, ensuring that rider experience considerations are clearly articulated, competitive and supported by data in tender and permit-related processes
 **.**



  





**WHAT YOU NEED TO EMBARK** 




 We are looking for a structured, analytical, and strategic problem-solver with the ability to balance user experience, compliance requirements, and commercial constraints across markets, and collaborate effectively with diverse stakeholders in a fast-paced environment. In addition to being fluent in English, we believe the right person has:
 



  




* A Bachelor's degrees within finance, economics, engineering or another related field
* 2+ years experience from operations, user experience and/or product optimisation
* Experience contributing to complex, multi-stakeholder performance improvement projects
* Hands-on experience of business modelling and data analysis
* Strong data analytics proficiency including ability to independently manage large data sets and provide strategic insights to key stakeholders
* Ability to build strong relationships, communicate effectively and manage key stakeholders
* Ability to support multiple projects simultaneou
 *sly*



  





***Don’t meet every requirement?***
*If you are excited about this role and our mission, and have relevant, transferable skills, we encourage you to apply - even if you don’t check every* 
 box.
 



  





**WHY VOI?** 




 Working at Voi is more than just a job; our People Promise includes a personal voyage where you will grow as a professional and be part of a team and culture that builds something meaningful for society. In addition to this, you’ll have the opportunity to:
 



  




* Join Europe’s #1 micromobility operator and one of the fastest-growing scaleups.
* Get “skin in the game” through our employee options program, and have a direct impact on Voi’s rider experience across all markets.
* Collaborate with inspiring, motivated, and fun colleagues towards a commongoal.
* Unlimited free Voirides.
* Join the micromobility revolution and help create sustainable cities made for living - free from noise and pollution.



  





**Are you ready to become a Voiager?** 




 Since 2018, Voi has been on a mission to create safe, sustainable and reliable micromobility for everyone. Today we are the leading shared micromobility operator in Europe, with HQ in Stockholm (Sweden), operating 150,000+ vehicles in 110+ towns and cities across 12 countries, serving 8+ million riders with over 400+ MILLION rides to date, and we have no intention of stopping here! We believe that micromobility will play a central role in changing how people move in our towns and cities in the future and want to ensure that the micromobility transformation happens the right way - through real innovative technology, partnership and transparent dialogue with towns, cities and governments, and by adapting our products to the local needs. In addition to improving quality of life in cities around the world, we are contributing to the solution for climate change by making sustainable transportation options more widely available and promoting renewable energy use and circularity along our supply chain.
 



  





**Diversity Matters** 




 As a Voiager, we offer you a friendly, collaborative and fast-paced workplace where you can bring your whole self to work, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, religion, disability or sexual orientation - and welcome applications from whoever you are, including veterans, those actively serving as a Reservist and their partners. What is most important to us is how you treat others and what skills and value you bring to our team. We believe that having a wide diversity of people with different backgrounds and perspectives within Voi is essential to our success in delivering the best user experience to our equally diverse user base. With employees from over 50 nationalities working within Voi, we believe we have made good progress but know we can still do more - therefore we discourage any photos, personal letters, or disclosure of any information that concerns other information than your professional experience.

title: Director of Ecommerce
description:
**About TOTEME** 




 TOTEME is a fashion house rooted in Swedish sensibilities, modern aspirations and the notion of style. With an appreciation for women’s many roles and how pieces are worn in practice, we create emblematic designs with an emphasis on materiality and shape. Representing a direct and decisive way of dressing, the collections are contextualized in curated edits, visuals and spaces.
 



  





 TOTEME was founded in 2014 by Elin Kling and Karl Lindman. From the atelier in Stockholm, we create collections spanning ready-to-wear, shoes, bags, accessories and jewelry.
 



  





**The Role** 




 TOTEME is looking for an E-commerce Director to join our team in Stockholm on parental leave cover from June 2026. As the E-commerce Director, you will be heading up the global E-commerce team at TOTEME, ensuring a commercially strong, innovative and on-brand e-commerce business. You will oversee the full customer journey from traffic acquisition, through conversion, site performance, front-end development, operations and innovation, to post-purchase experience, ensuring a locally relevant and scalable business. You will also oversee the development of our global marketplace business and be a key commercial stakeholder amongst Brand-Marketing & Communications teams. You will report to the Chief Commercial Officer.
 



  





**Responsibilities** 



* Lead and develop the global e-commerce team consisting of fifteen employees, of which five are direct reports, across digital merchandising, performance marketing, e-commerce product, digital creative, operations and customer service
* Responsible for the global E-commerce P&L and Farfetch P&L
* Own and drive TOTEME’s global e-commerce strategy and roadmap, strengthening the brand’s position in the international digital fashion landscape
* Overall responsibility for the performance and continuous development of toteme.com, ensuring a premium, end-to-end luxury customer journey, as well as global marketplace (Farfetch) & affiliate performance
* Drive traffic growth and digital marketing performance in line with ROAS targets across global and local channels (paid social, SEO, SEM, paid shopping, Google, Meta, affiliates and local platforms)
* Ensure continuous development and optimization of e-commerce platforms and the e-com tech/app stack (Shopify, partner websites and apps) in collaboration with internal teams and external partners
* Deliver an on-brand on-site experience while optimizing conversion rates and reducing return rates through UX/UI development, testing and data-driven insights
* Lead locally relevant merchandising and shopping experiences across markets
* Be a key stakeholder in campaign and seasonal planning alongside Merchandising, Buying, Marketing and Brand
* Own the post-purchase experience, including delivery services, returns and customer service
* Manage the e-commerce budget, digital infrastructure and external partners to enable scalable, cost-efficient and high-quality global operations
* Participate in and contribute to TOTEME’s social and environmental sustainability strategy.



  





**Your Profile** 



* Proven experience in delivering a cohesive, on-brand e-commerce experience within the luxury or premium designer fashion segment, with a strong understanding of how digital touchpoints strengthen the brand positioning and representation
* Leadership experience and the ability to inspire, motivate, and challenge teams, creating clarity, engagement, and commitment across functions and levels of the organisation.
* Proven commercial ownership of e-commerce performance, including revenue growth, profitability, and contribution to overall business targets.
* Experience setting and executing a long-term e-commerce strategy aligned with brand positioning and business growth.
* Deep understanding of key e-commerce areas such as traffic, conversion, site operations, site performance, front-end development and innovation
* Solid knowledge of e-commerce platforms and a good understanding of underlying IT architecture and integrations.
* Proven ability to lead cross-functionally and collaborate with both internal, and external stakeholders and team.
* Experience working with multibrand and online marketplaces, with the ability to manage and optimise third-party marketplace operations alongside a direct-to-consumer e-commerce business.



  





**About You** 



* Solution-oriented and hands-on, with the ability to move seamlessly between strategic direction and operational execution.
* Commercially driven and results-focused, with a strong understanding of how e-commerce performance supports broader business objectives.
* Data-driven and digitally savvy, able to interpret insights and analytics to inform decisions, prioritisation, and continuous optimisation.
* Self-motivated, accountable, and confident in decision-making, with a strong sense of ownership.
* Innovation-led, with a proactive interest in exploring new technologies and ways of working to enhance performance and customer experience.



  





 This is a parental leave cover for minimum 8 months from June 2026, with possible extension. It is a full-time position based at TOTEME's headquarters in Stockholm with start upon agreement.
 



  





 TOTEME has teams in Stockholm, London, New York, and Shanghai, as well as retail spaces across Europe, North America, and Asia. As a member of our dynamic, creative, and highly collaborative team, you will take part in exciting projects and milestones, evolving within your role as you contribute to the company’s growth.
 



  





 TOTEME stands for inclusivity and a sense of belonging in the workplace. We welcome all applicants and recruit on the basis of skills and experience.
 



  





*By applying to this position I agree with TOTEME’s privacy policy and to be contacted about future job opportunities for up to 2 years from the submission date. Find out more about our privacy policy
 
 here
 
 .*

title: CPO till ClearOn
description:
Vill du vara med och leda produktutvecklingen i ett dynamiskt företag som står inför spännande tillväxtmöjligheter? Vi söker en erfaren och strategisk ledare till rollen som CPO (Head of Product) till ClearOn.
   

  

 Som CPO kommer du att ansvara för att forma och genomföra bolagets produktvision och leda ett team bestående av produktchefer och produktägare. Du kommer att spela en nyckelroll i ClearOn:s fortsatta tillväxt och ingå i företagets ledningsgrupp.
   

  

 I rollen kommer du att driva produktvisionen på ledningsnivå och samarbeta tätt med CEO och ledningsgruppen för att utveckla och förverkliga företagets strategi. Du är ansvarig för hela produktportföljen, inklusive affärsutveckling och innovation, med fokus på att säkerställa ClearOns kommersiella framgång och fortsatta utveckling.
   

  

 ClearOn:s kontor är beläget i Liljeholmen och du kommer rapportera till CEO.
   

  

**Dina huvudsakliga ansvarsområden:**
* Definiera och genomföra en produktvision i linje med företagets mål och affärsstrategi.
* Ansvara för ClearOns produkter och tjänster samt deras P&L.
* Leda och coacha ett team av produktchefer och produktägare för att främja en kultur av innovation och samarbete.
* Ansvara för att produktvision och roadmap skall möta dagens och morgondagens behov.
* Säkerställa effektiv kommunikation mellan olika avdelningar och produktteamet.
* Hantera produktutvecklingsprocesser för att säkerställa högkvalitativ och tidsenlig leverans.


**Kvalifikationer:**
* Minst 5 års erfarenhet inom produktledning, varav minst 2 år med ledarskap över produktchefer.
* Starka ledarskapsförmågor och erfarenhet av att inspirera och motivera team i snabbrörliga tekniska miljöer.
* Gedigen erfarenhet inom produktutveckling, marknadsföring och djup förståelse för SaaS-lösningar.
* Erfarenhet av datadriven produktutveckling, A/B-testning och användarundersökningar.
* Kunskap om agila metoder och SCRUM-ramverket.
* Meriterande om du har erfarenhet inom FMCG.


**Vi söker dig som:**
* Har förmåga att översätta marknadsbehov till strategiska produktplaner och balansera kortsiktiga och långsiktiga mål.
* Är lösningsorienterad, drivande och målinriktad med en stark förmåga att anpassa dig till förändringar.


**Om ClearOn**
 ClearOn är Sveriges marknadsledande partner för säljdrivande kampanjer både i butik och digital närvaro inom dagligvaru- och servicehandeln med över 50 års erfarenhet.
   

  

 Vi är en full servicepartner inom säljdrivande lösningar där vi erbjuder innovativa tekniska lösningar och interaktiva kampanjer som skapar engagemang och försäljning i butik.
   

  

 Vi arbetar utifrån ett 360 graders perspektiv och erbjuder strategisk rådgivning och analys och driver långsiktig tillväxt genom unika och konkurrenskraftiga erbjudanden och tjänster i strävan att nå vårt mål som en komplett partner för att förstå, aktivera och sammanföra varumärken med konsumenter.
   

  

**Frågor och kontakt**
 Vi träffar kandidater löpande och ser fram emot att ta del av din ansökan. Under jul- och nyårsperioden kan återkoppling ta lite längre tid än vanligt. Tack för ditt tålamod!
   

  

 I denna rekrytering samarbetar ClearOn med Amendo Tech. Vid frågor om tjänsten är du välkommen att kontakta Johanna Hallenberg på johanna.hallenberg@amendotech.se. Vi ser fram emot att höra från dig!
   

  

**Om Amendo Tech**
 Amendo Tech är det självklara konsult- och rekryteringsföretaget inom tech, IT och digital utveckling.
   

  

 Amendo Tech är en del av Amendo Group som har arbetat med kompetensförsörjning sedan 2001. I koncernen ingår även Lagotto Executive Search & Interim som är specialiserade på executive search och interima tillsättningar av ledare och specialister, samt Amendo Bemanning och Rekrytering som erbjuder rekryterings- och konsultlösningar inom ekonomi, bank, finans, försäkring, HR och kommunikation.
   

  

 Följ gärna Amendo Tech på Linkedin.
   

  

 #IT

title: Principal Software Engineer
description:
**Role:**  

Principal Software Engineer – Platform Owner (Hands-on)
  
  

**Context:**  

At Furhat Robotics we build social humanoid robots that talk, listen, and interact with people in the real world.
  
  

Our robots are already used by universities, research labs, hospitals, and innovation teams around the world to study and deploy human–AI interaction.
  
  

This isn’t a prototype or a pitch deck.
  
It’s a real, shipped product running globally.
  
  

**Technically, it’s a rare mix of:**  

hardware
  
realtime systems
  
SDKs
  
cloud services
  
AI/LLMs
  
developer tools
  
  

We’re profitable and small (~20 people).
  
  

We’re not trying to build a big engineering organization.
  
  

We want one very strong engineer who can own the whole platform.
  
  

**The role (plainly):**  

You will take full ownership of engineering.
  
  

Not manage it.
  
Not coordinate it.
  
Own it.
  
  

If something breaks, ships late, or feels messy — it’s yours to fix.
  
  

If things run smoothly — you did your job.
  
  

You’ll work directly with the CEO, our CSO (research), and our product manager.
  
  

We have two junior consultants today. You’ll lead them and decide how we build going forward.
  
  

**What you’ll actually do:**  

Expect 80–90% hands-on coding/building/fixing.
  
  

This is not a management role.
  
  

**You will:**  

Own the robot platform (Java)
  
Own the SDK (Kotlin)
  
Own internal tools (React)
  
Own APIs and backend services
  
Own cloud & DevOps (AWS/Azure)
  
Run CI/CD and releases
  
Clean up legacy and simplify architecture
  
Break roadmap into shipped features
  
Guide/mentor junior engineers and consultants
  
Hire very selectively when truly needed
  
  

When engineering is slow or fragile, you fix the system — not add headcount.
  
  

**What success looks like:**  

**After 6–12 months:**  

Releases are predictable
  
Shipping is faster
  
Fewer fires
  
Cleaner architecture
  
Consultants need less babysitting
  
Furhat engineering has momentum and continues to lead with software capabilities
  
Skills & Requirements
  
  

**Who this is for:**  

**You probably:**  

Are excited about AI and robotics
  
Are excited about building a reliable platform developers (specifically researchers) love to use
  
Have been a founding engineer, principal engineer, or startup CTO before
  
Have taken messy systems and made them reliable
  
Like owning things end-to-end
  
Prefer small teams over big orgs
  
Are comfortable working independently
  
Don’t need a lot of process or meetings
  
Like coding more than managing
  
Solve problems with automation and simplification, not hiring
  
Communicate clearly and take initiative
  
  

You don’t need robotics experience.
  
  

Strong backend/platform experience is more important.
  
  

**Who this is NOT for:**  

**This will not be a good fit if you:**  

Want to build or manage a large engineering org
  
Prefer delegating over coding
  
Need lots of structure or process to make progress
  
Want a big team around you
  
Like titles more than ownership
  
  

We intentionally keep the team small and senior.
  
  

**Tech stack:**  

Java (robot platform)
  
Kotlin/Python (SDK)
  
React (frontend)
  
C++ (computer vision/audio)
  
APIs / backend services
  
AWS + Azure
  
GitHub + CI/CD
  
Linux based OS
  
Some AI/LLM integrations
  
  

**Practical details:**  

Stockholm (onsite)
  
Small team
  
Direct access to founders
  
Competitive Salary
  
Meaningful equity
  
Real ownership and autonomy
  
  

This role is closer to being a technical co-founder than a typical employee.
  
  

If this sounds exciting rather than scary, we should talk.
  
  

-
  
  

**About us:**  

Furhat Robotics builds social humanoid robots that interact with people face-to-face.
  
  

Our robots are already deployed in universities, research labs, hospitals, and innovation teams across Europe, the US, and Asia.
  
  

**They’re used to:**  

study human, AI interaction
  
teach and train
  
prototype AI assistants
  
explore how people will work with embodied AI systems
  
  

This isn’t research code or a demo.
  
  

It’s a real hardware + software platform with paying customers, used daily around the world.
  
  

We’re a small (~20 people), profitable company focused on building a solid product, not chasing hypergrowth or headcount.
  
  

If you like working on something tangible and technically deep, robotics, realtime systems, SDKs, cloud, AI, this is that rare mix.
  
  

Great Place to Work
  
  

Flexible hours
  
  

Competitive option package
  
  

Paid vacation & parental leave
  
  

Monthly workout grants
  
  

Personal health insurance & life insurance
  
  

Free beverages, snacks, team ginger shots
  
  

Torsgatan 39, close to Sankt Eriksplan
  
  

Breakfast
  
  

Team building. Game nights, AWs, cook-offs etc.
  
  

Easily accessible for commuters
  
  

Work with a technology that will change the world
  
  

About the company
  
  

We are an artificial intelligence and social robotics startup with the vision to make interaction with technology truly human. Founded by four PhDs in social robotics at KTH, we are an ambitious, small team of scientists, engineers and entrepreneurs backed by prominent investors. Our robots are the most expressive and customizable social robots on the market, powered by a state-of-the-art multi-modal conversational platform and a developer suite used by 3rd party developers to build robot applications. We are now looking for high-achievers that share our passion, to join our ride.
  
  

Enable "functional cookies" to watch the video
  
  

Samer Al Moubayed, CEO | Contact Person

title: Backend Engineer
description:
At Embark, you will join a small team working on backend services that power player-facing features and online systems at scale. In this role, you will work with social and account related backend systems, enabling features such as voice chat, messaging, parties, and player connectivity. These systems handle identity, communication, and social interaction between players across our games.
  
The challenges you will solve are similar to those found in large-scale communication and identity platforms, but applied to games.
  
You will work in distributed systems running in production. Most of your time will be spent in a small number of core services, while understanding how they connect to the larger system.
  
Because the team is small, you are expected to take responsibility, ask questions and identify what needs to be done.
  
Our stack includes Go, C++, SQL using Google Spanner, Redis, GCP, Kubernetes, and Bazel.
  
  

Example of responsibilities
  
  

Design, build, and maintain social and account backend services
  
  

Work with distributed systems in production
  
  

Collaborate with backend, game server, and infrastructure teams to build reliable online systems
  
  

Improve tooling, workflows, and architecture when needed
  
  

It would be awesome if you have
  
  

Strong programming skills and professional experience with Go
  
  

Experience working with SQL based systems, Spanner is a strong plus
  
  

Experience with distributed systems
  
  

Experience with Kubernetes and cloud environments, preferably GCP
  
  

The ability to take ownership and drive work forward
  
  

A curious mindset and comfort working in complex systems
  
  

Professional English communication skills
  
  

Bonus
  
  

Experience building or operating authentication systems using OpenID or similar standards
  
  

Experience with C++
  
  

At Embark we offer competitive salaries, a generous profit-sharing program, and much more, but most of all we invite you to take part of a journey into the unknown, to build creative, surprising and beautiful experiences together.
  
We welcome people from all backgrounds and are looking forward to reading more about you (in English)!

title: Senior Kravanalytiker IT
description:
Organisationsbeskrivning
  
  

För kundens räkning utvecklar och förvaltar digitala tjänster som används av många människor i Sverige. Arbetet sker i moderna IT-miljöer där flera agila team samarbetar för att utveckla och förbättra digitala lösningar.
  
  

Arbetsgivarbeskrivning
  
  

Vi är ett IT-konsultbolag som levererar specialistkompetens inom systemutveckling, kravanalys och digital utveckling. Våra konsulter arbetar i komplexa IT-miljöer där kvalitet, struktur och samarbete är centrala delar av arbetet.
  
  

Jobbeskrivning
  
  

Vi söker upp till fyra erfarna kravanalytiker inom IT till ett längre konsultuppdrag i Sundsvall med möjlighet till hybridarbete.
  
  

Du kommer att arbeta i agila utvecklingsteam med nyutveckling, vidareutveckling och förvaltning av organisationens IT-system. Rollen innebär nära samarbete med verksamhet, utvecklingsteam och produktägare.
  
  

**Plats:** Sundsvall
  
**Distansarbete:** 60–80 % möjligt
  
**Omfattning:** Heltid
  
**Start:** snarast möjligt, senast 2026-08-03
  
**Uppdrag:** till 2027-06-30 med möjlighet till förlängning
  
**Antal konsulter:** upp till 4
  
  

Arbetsuppgifter
  
  

Analysera verksamhetsbehov
  
Formulera systemkrav
  
Ta fram epics och user stories
  
Definiera acceptanskriterier
  
Aäkerställa spårbarhet i krav
  
Arbeta med backlogg tillsammans med produktägare
  
Delta i sprintplanering och andra agila möten
  
Arbeta i verktyg som Jira och Confluence
  
  

Kvalifikationskrav
  
Minst 5 års erfarenhet som kravanalytiker inom IT
  
Erfarenhet av agil systemutveckling
  
Erfarenhet av kravanalys och dokumentation
  
Erfarenhet av epics och user stories
  
God samarbetsförmåga och strukturerat arbetssätt
  
Flytande svenska i tal och skrift
  
Arbetet ska utföras från Sverige
  
CV på svenska ska bifogas
  
Minst två referenser ska kunna lämnas
  
  

Meriterande
  
Erfarenhet från större IT-organisationer
  
Erfarenhet från offentlig verksamhet
  
Microservices eller eventdriven arkitektur
  
Containerplattformar som Kubernetes
  
Testarbete i utvecklingsprojekt
  
Erfarenhet av Jira
  
  

Ansökan
  
  

**Skicka in:**  

CV på svenska
  
Referensuppdrag enligt bilaga
  
**Sista ansökningsdag:** 12 mars 2026
