title: Fraud Analyst
description:
*Are you driven by analytics, automation, and stopping fraud before it happens? Join our fintech bank and help us design the next generation of data-driven fraud prevention in Sweden.*
 As a Fraud Analyst, you will work at the intersection of data science, risk, and real-time decisioning. You will develop, test, and optimize fraud detection models across lending, cards, and payments. Your analyses will directly influence risk appetite, customer experience, and portfolio performance.
   

  

 You will:
   

  

* Analyze large, complex datasets to detect fraud patterns and emerging attack vectors
* Develop and maintain fraud models, rules, and monitoring frameworks
* Build dashboards and reporting that translate risk signals into actionable insight
* Partner closely with Fraud Prevention, Credit Underwriting, IT, and Data Engineering
* Continuously improve automation, decision engines, and model performance


 This is a hands-on role in a fast-moving environment where your work directly impacts loss rates, approval rates, and customer trust.
   

  

**Who You Are**
* 5+ years’ experience in data science, analytics, or quantitative risk roles within a bank, fintech or related industry
* Strong knowledge of machine learning, statistical modeling, and model validation
* Advanced SQL and Python (R is a plus)
* Experience with BI and visualization tools (e.g. Power BI, Tableau, Qlik, SAS)
* Comfortable working with large datasets and production-grade decision systems
* Able to translate complex analysis into clear, business-driven recommendations
* Experience in financial fraud prevention or fraud risk management is highly meriting


 You are analytical, commercially minded, and motivated by solving adversarial problems in real time. You thrive in a fintech environment where speed, ownership, and impact matter.
   

  

**Resurs** 
**in brief**
 At Resurs, we make everyday finances easier through innovative and customer-focused solutions. With customers across the Nordics and more than 800 colleagues, we are committed to creating long-term value for individuals, businesses, and society. Recognized as a career company for the past six years, we put strong emphasis on both personal and professional growth. We want to make a difference by striving to be our best, acting with kindness, and building trust through clarity and collaboration. Our heart beats strong and it shows in everything we do.
   

  

 Although we are based in the Nordics, our team is enriched by the breadth of perspectives brought by colleagues from different countries. We foster a welcoming and supportive culture, where collaboration drives us to achieve the best outcomes for our users.
   

  

**Apply today!**
 Don’t wait to send in your application. Selection and interviews take place on an ongoing basis, and the position may be filled before the last application date.
   

  

 We look forward to hearing from you!
   

  

**Important information**
 On final candidates, we carry out a detailed background check via an external company.
   

  

 Resurs has almost 50 years of experience in retail finance and is one of the leading niche banks in the Nordics. We help companies and private individuals with lending, savings and payments. Today, we have a customer base of slightly more than 6 million customers and around 800 employees in the Nordic region.
   

  

 Follow us on Instagram:
   

  

 Resurs.Sverige
   

  

 Resurs.Norge
   

  

 Resurs.Suomi
   

  

 Resurs.Danmark

title: Security Analyst 24/7
description:
Vi på Iver brinner för innovationer och god laganda, vi är särskilt intresserad av dig som gillar att tänka nytt och dela med dig av dina tankar. Vi söker en
 **Security Analyst** 
**24/7** 
 som vill bidra till bättre säkrare IT-miljör för våra kunder. Är du vår nya kollega?
   

  

**We empower our Nordic society**
 Iver är en ledande nordisk helhetsleverantör av molnbaserade IT-tjänster med spetskompetens inom drift, cybersäkerhet, cloud och innovation. Våra tjänster är kritiska inte bara för våra kunder utan för digitaliseringen av samhället i stort. Med huvudkontor i Stockholm verkar Iver främst i Norden med en agenda att fortsätta expandera. Iver omsätter drygt 3 miljarder SEK och har cirka 1 300 anställda som arbetar på ett av våra 23 kontor i Norden.
   

  

**Din roll på Iver:**
 Som säkerhetsanalytiker kommer du att vara en viktig del av vårt skiftteam som arbetar dygnet runt. Du ansvarar för kontinuerlig övervakning av säkerhetslarm och genomför inledande utredningar av potentiella incidenter. Du följer etablerade procedurer för att bedöma och eskalera händelser vid behov, vilket säkerställer snabb kommunikation och support till våra kunder.
   

  

 I nära samarbete med seniora analytiker kommer du att utveckla dina färdigheter inom incidenthantering och få praktisk erfarenhet i ett tempofyllt säkerhetsoperationscenter. Rollen ger möjlighet att lära av erfarna teammedlemmar, bidra till tillförlitlig övervakning och säkerställa stark säkerhet för våra kunder dygnet runt.
   

  

**Exempel på uppgifter:**
 Övervaka kundmiljöer, säkerhetsplattformar och varningar för att upptäcka potentiella incidenter, eskalera och vägleda kunder genom respons och åtgärd.
   

  

**Vad erbjuder vi dig?**
 På Iver får du möjlighet att arbeta med den senaste tekniken och vara med på en spännande tillväxtresa. Vi är inkluderande och strävar efter ett genuint samarbete mot gemensamma mål. Inom Iver finns stora möjligheter till utveckling och vi finns här för att inspirera dig till att nå dina yrkesmässiga mål. Vi står bakom dig när du vill lära dig mer inom ditt specialområde, ledarskap eller nya områden. Hos Iver är du också en del av ett ambitiöst och innovativt företag, vilket innebär att förbättra, testa, utvärdera och våga ändra hur vi gör saker är viktiga ingredienser för att vi ska utvecklas.
   

  

 Vi på Iver ger samma löfte till våra kollegor som till våra kunder. Löftet är att alltid vara ”beside you”, ”behind you” och ”before you”. Vi kallar det för "The Iver way".
   

  

**Förmåner hos oss:**
 📄 Kollektivavtal & trygga villkor
   

  

 💪 Friskvårdsbidrag
   

  

 👓 Terminalglasögon
   

  

 ⏳ Tidsbank – 24 h extra ledighet/år
   

  

 🩺 Privat sjukvårdsförsäkring
   

  

 💼 Tjänstepension (ITP) & flexpension
   

  

**Vilka söker vi?**
 Du är nyfiken, engagerad och vill utvecklas inom området. Du trivs med att samarbeta, är kommunikativ, strukturerad och har en ödmjuk inställning till att lära dig nytt. Du har ett serviceinriktat arbetssätt och tycker om att bidra till lösningar tillsammans med andra. Vi söker dig som har cirka två års erfarenhet inom området och som vill ta nästa steg i din karriär. Vi ser gärna att du har intresse för, eller grundläggande kunskap inom, några av följande områden:
   

  

* EDR/XDR/SIEM-teknik – Du har arbetat med dessa lösningar och förstår hur de används i praktiken.
* Automatisering – Du har insikt i hur automatisering kan effektivisera arbetet och förbättra kvaliteten, gärna med erfarenhet av SOAR-lösningar.
* Kunddialog – Du är van att kommunicera med olika typer av kunder, kan ge rekommendationer och föreslå lösningar utifrån deras behov.


**Plats:** 
 Iver har 25 platser i Norden. För denna roll ser vi att du kommer att vara baserad på ett av våra kontor i Stockholm.
   

  

**Övrig information:** 
 Vi har kunder inom alla branscher och sektorer, inklusive säkerhetsklassad verksamhet, vilket är anledningen till att vi utför bakgrundskontroller i alla våra rekryteringsprocesser.
   

  

 Ansök till oss! Urval sker löpande, tjänsten kan tillsättas innan ansökningsdatum. Iver strävar efter könsbalans och värdesätter etnisk och kulturell mångfald. Önskad start är så snart som möjligt eller enligt överenskommelse.
   

  

**Ansökningsdag:** 
 2026-04-19
   

  

**Start:** 
 Enligt överenskommelse
   

  

 Välkommen att kontakta Thandi Schüler (thandi.schuler@iver.se) om du har några frågor eller funderingar kring tjänsten.
   

  

 Vi ser fram emot att ta emot din ansökan!
   

  

**We empower our Nordic society** 
 . Iver är en ledande nordisk helhetsleverantör av molnbaserade IT-tjänster med spetskompetens inom drift, cybersäkerhet, cloud och innovation.
   

  

**Våra tjänster är kritiska inte bara för våra kunder – utan för digitaliseringen av samhället i stort.** 
 Vi säkerställer att sjukvården har tillgång till rätt patientdata, att samhällskritiska system aldrig ligger nere och att flygplanen landar säkert. Som en lyhörd och affärsnära helhetspartner möjliggör vi våra kunders innovation – idag och imorgon.

title: Commercial Engineer
description:
Voyado is the ultimate solution for personalization and relevance across all channels - online and offline. Trusted by retailers and e-commerce companies worldwide, the Voyado Customer Experience Cloud empowers brands to engage customers, elevate shopping experiences, and grow their businesses.
   

  

**About The Role**
 As a Commercial Engineer at Voyado, you'll step into an exciting role at the intersection of technology, sales, partnerships
 **,** 
 and strategy. Initially supporting the presales of our product Elevate, you’ll combine technical expertise with commercial acumen to drive our sales efforts and support our partners. You’ll play a pivotal role in understanding customer needs, delivering compelling product demonstrations, and tailoring solutions that address client challenges.
   

  

 If you thrive on blending tech-savvy insights with commercial problem-solving, this is your chance to shine!
   

  

 What you'll be up to
   

  

* Collaborate with the sales team and partners to identify customer needs and deliver tailored technical solutions.
* Conduct in-depth product demonstrations and technical presentations to showcase the capabilities of Elevate, and further down the line, also our product Engage.
* Address technical challenges, customizing solutions to ensure alignment with client requirements.
* Act as a bridge between sales, partner managers, and product teams, translating technical details into clear business benefits.
* Stay updated on product enhancements, industry trends, and the competitive landscape.


**About You**
 We’re looking for a dynamic professional ready to take the next step in their career. Ideally, you have a technical background and a business-oriented mindset, enjoy problem-solving, thrive in a collaborative environment, and have a knack for translating complex technical details into compelling value propositions.
   

  

*What we’re looking for:*
* Proven experience as a Sales Engineer, Solution Engineer, in Pre-sales or a technical customer-facing role, such as working with implementing a SaaS Solution.
* Background working with complex digital products, preferably SaaS solutions.
* Ability to bridge business and technical conversations, translating API and integration concepts into commercial value for different stakeholders.
* Good communication and presentation skills, with the ability to simplify and engage.
* Creative problem-solving skills to address diverse customer needs.


 Experience in the e-commerce industry is highly meritorious (and experience with Voyado specifically is a strong plus).
   

  

 What's in it for you?
   

  

 Voyado is a Swedish purpose-driven company with nearly 400 Voyadoers, where every opinion is recognized and where you’ll be successful when putting the customers first. Here, you'll get the opportunity to work self-driven with strong support that you can leverage at any time. We really care for our Voyadoers and put a lot of time and effort into keeping and nurturing our knowledge-sharing and friendly culture. We’re personal and that extends into everything about us. Read more on what it’s like to work at Voyado
 **here** 
 .
   

  

**What We Offer:**
* Regular team summits for bonding, workshops, and knowledge-sharing
* Wellness allowance & wellness hour to focus on your well-being
* Competitive pension plan
* 30 paid vacation days + local national holidays
* Weekly team breakfasts
* Personal gadget budget - Choose the tech that works for you
* Voyado+ perks program


 More on Voyado!
   

  

 We are a purpose-driven company where every opinion is valued, and every team member has the opportunity to make a meaningful impact. At Voyado, you’ll work in a self-driven environment with strong support from your team.
   

  

 Ready to find out more?
   

  

 Lovely! 🎉
   

  

 Before one applies, it can always feel like you need a better resume, more polish, or just another year of experience, but if you think this sounds like a place where you can shine, don't hesitate to send in your resume!
   

  

 We'd love to get to know you and understand what makes you tick, so write a few lines about what you enjoy about your current role and what would be an exciting challenge for you in the future. We are eager to get to know you and what you love to do, so be yourself and let your passion shine through in your application!
   

  

 Additional information
   

  

 As part of our recruitment process, we conduct a background check on the final candidate. We use background checks from Scandinavian Recruitment Intelligence. Here you can read more about how a background check is done.
   

  

**Making it personal with AI**
 We truly believe you’ll position yourself best for success in the interview process by being yourself with support from AI, where it makes sense. Later in the process, you will even be expected to use AI. But at the end of the day, what matters is meeting you, the person behind the application.
   

  

 There is no such thing as a perfect candidate; we’re all human, so please, bring your authentic self! Don’t let AI over-polish your CV or answers to the point where we can’t hear your real voice, because that’s what we care about the most.
   

  

 Voyado is the customer experience platform made for retail. We help brands turn every interaction—from first click to repeat purchase—into lasting loyalty.
   

  

 With AI-powered tools for search, personalization, marketing, and loyalty, we make it easy for retailers to grow and connect with customers in smarter ways.
   

  

 Loved by 400+ brands, Voyado is on a mission to become Europe’s most beloved retail companion.

title: Cyber Security (IAM) Analyst
description:
**We are looking of a Cyber Security (IAM) Analyst for a global company in Stockholm. Start is in April, 7-month limited contract to begin with.** 




  





  





**Key Responsibilities** 



* Lead integration activities between HR systems and Identity Governance & Administration (IGA) tools
* Act as Subject Matter Expert (SME) between HR and IAM teams
* Lead IAM-related initiatives for Fieldglass (internal HR tool)
* Support the Enterprise IAM Transformation Program
* Ensure alignment between HR processes and IAM governance frameworks



  





  





**Requirements** 



* Senior-level experience in IAM and HR system integrations
* Strong understanding of HR-to-IAM/IGA tool integrations
* Solid knowledge of IAM processes, including joiner-mover-leaver (JML), access governance, and role management
* Experience working cross-functionally between business and technical teams



  





  





**This role requires fluency in Swedish and English, both written and spoken.** 




  





  





 This is a full-time consultant position in Stockholm through Incluso. Start is in April, 7-month limited contract to begin with, with possibility of extension after that. This role is 100% onsite in Stockholm.
 



  





  





 Please submit your CV as soon as possible since we will review the applications on an on-going basis.
 



  





  





 For more information about this role, please contact Marianne Nilsson recruiter at Incluso.

title: Solutions Specialist Palo Alto
description:
**Do you want to be at the forefront of protecting our society against cyber threats? We are looking for an L2 Analyst – Escalation & Platform Specialist to team up with the most dedicated team of cyber specialists in our Managed Security Services.**
**About Truesec – A Leading Cybersecurity Company**
 Since 2005, Truesec has been a native cybersecurity company, driven by one single purpose: Creating safety and sustainability in a digital world by preventing cyber breach and minimizing impact.
   

  

 Over the years, we’ve earned the trust of organizations worldwide and gained a strong reputation internationally. Our team consists of dedicated specialists covering the entire spectrum of cybersecurity – with capabilities in Predict, Prevent, Protect, Respond, and Recover.
   

  

 Today, we employ around 400 employees – and as cybercrime grows, we need to grow. To support that goal, we are now looking for an L2 Analyst – Escalation & Platform Specialist
 **,** 
 who wants to make a difference in our Managed Security Services business unit.
   

  

**The Role**
 Join our Detection Solutions team as a Solution Specialist in the Palo Alto and SentinelOne Security Suits, where you’ll take ownership of the technical landscape across Palo Alto and SentinelOne security products. This hybrid role blends technical cyber security expertise with both internal and external enablement, ensuring our Palo Alto and SentinelOne security platforms are optimally configured, up-to-date, and fully leveraged by our SOC teams and our customers. In this role, you will:
   

  

* Have the responsibility for our technical platforms for Palo Alto and SentinelOne security solutions, focused on EDR, ITDR, SIEM, and related security components.
* Monitor, assess, and communicate updates, new features, and licensing changes from the vendors to ensure our security capabilities remain cutting-edge.
* Enable and educate - share your expertise by keeping SOC analysts and internal stakeholders up to date on the latest platform developments, detection opportunities, and best practices. Translate complex updates into actionable guidance and ensure knowledge is embedded in our processes.
* Work closely with the vendor representatives, engineering teams, and internal stakeholders to troubleshoot issues, optimize deployments, and increase detection and response capabilities, representing the Detection Solutions team with clarity and credibility.
* Drive ongoing enhancements across our Palo Alto and SentinelOne security tooling and processes, ensuring that new platform capabilities are rapidly and effectively integrated.


**Your Potential Background**
 We are looking for someone with a passion for cybersecurity, a strong sense of product ownership, and a collaborative mindset. You thrive in a dynamic environment, are eager to stay current with the latest XDR technologies and enjoy sharing your expertise with others. You have 2-5 years of hands-on experience in cybersecurity, ideally as a product specialist within Palo Alto’s or SentinelOne’s Security Suits, Technical Account Manager, or similar roles.
   

  

**Required Skills:**
* Deep familiarity with XDR Security Suits, including deployment strategies and advanced technical capabilities within XDR and log.
* Experience with operationalizing XDR security solutions.
* Strong interest in technical product ownership, with a proactive approach to learning and staying current with evolving security features and best practices.
* Ability to communicate complex technical concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
* Collaborative and outgoing personality; comfortable representing the team in cross-functional meetings, vendor dialogues, and technical customer discussions.
* Self-motivated, with a proven ability to work independently.


 Due to the sensitivity in our assignments, we require Swedish citizenship, citizenship in an EU country, or a Swedish work permit.
   

  

**Join Our Mission Toward a Safe Digital Future**
 If you truly want to make a difference and make our world a little bit safer, this is the place for you. At Truesec, you’ll get to be at the forefront of fighting cybercrime instead of reading about it in the news. This is your chance to be part of an exciting journey in a leading cybersecurity company – and learn from some of the best in their field.
   

  

 So, are you ready to team up with the most dedicated team of experts and be part of a meaningful mission? Then we are excited to get to know you!

title: Finance Associate
description:
**About Antler**
 Antler is a global early-stage VC firm that partners with exceptional founders from day zero to build impactful, scalable companies.We believe exceptional companies are built by exceptional people - often before an idea, team, or company exists. Our role is to find those people early, surround them with the right co-founders, capital, and conviction, and help them build ambitious, venture-scale businesses from scratch.
   

  

 Founded in 2017, Antler now operates in 27 cities across six continents. Globally, we’ve helped create and invest in over 1,500 companies (including Lovable, Airalo and Peec AI) and supported more than 10,000 founders - with the ambition to back thousands more over the coming decade.
   

  

 In the Nordics, we are building one of the most active and hands-on early-stage investment firms in the ecosystem, backing founders from inception through to scale.
   

  

 The Role
   

  

 Behind every investment decision, every fund close, and every founder we support is a finance function that makes it all work here in the Nordics. You will be a part of a two-person team running the financial infrastructure that lets us move fast, make good decisions, and keep our investors and founders confident.
   

  

 We are looking for a Finance Associate to support our finance operations across the Nordics. Working closely with the Finance Manager and local Partner group, you'll handle financial workflows across our Nordic operating companies and two funds — covering everything from day-to-day accounting to tax, legal, and regulatory compliance.
   

  

 This is not a back-office role. You'll have real ownership from day one, working directly with our Nordic Partners and plugging into Antler's global finance team on reporting, fund operations, and everything in between.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Own monthly management accounts, delivering detailed variance analysis against budget
* Support on annual budgeting and forecasting, supporting strategic financial planning
* Analyse fund performance KPIs and produce quarterly NAV management accounts
* Support the Manager and General Partners with treasury, working capital, cash flow, and supplier payments
* Lead payroll, employer social costs, and corporate and VAT tax compliance across Nordic entities
* Support annual filings for operating entities, the management company, and the fund, and coordinate the annual audit process with external auditors
* Ensure timely reporting to investors, regulators, tax authorities, and other stakeholders, support global group consolidation requirements, and drive automation and process improvement initiatives
* Support group consolidation and drive automation and process improvement initiatives


**Requirements**
* At least one year of experience in finance, accounting, or business management
* Comfortable with ambiguity — you can own a task from start to finish without needing a playbook
* Detail-oriented and process-driven, with strong quantitative instincts
* Highly organised: you can keep multiple workflows moving simultaneously without dropping the ball
* A proactive communicator who's as comfortable presenting to Partners as writing a clear Slack message
* An eye for improvements, ideally you already implement AI into your workflows and are obsessed about doing things smarter
* Experience in VC, fund accounting, portfolio valuations, or legal agreements is a bonus — but not a dealbreaker


 What we offer
   

  

* Real ownership and accountability from day one and a steep learning curve working across fund operations, tax, legal, and investor relations
* Direct exposure to experienced investors, founders, and operators across the Nordic and global Antler network
* A front-row seat to the early-stage startup ecosystem
* A collaborative, high-trust team that values initiative, clear thinking, and a good sense of humor
* Flexible and international working environment, based in Stockholm


 Powered by JazzHR
   

  

 j2dnDjbCJo

title: Cloud Engineer/Architect
description:
Do you have a genuine interest in how business change, new technologies, evolving cloud and infrastructure solutions that help shape the IT landscape?
   

  

 Software & Platform Engineering – your new department:
   

  

 You will become part of a welcoming team of more than 40 highly skilled and engaged colleagues across Sweden. As part of Accenture Technology, a community of 400+ professionals, we collaborate across industries and bring together the right mix of skills for each unique assignment.
   

  

 Our strength lies in the diversity of our experience across technologies, methods, and perspectives and in how we support one another. This makes us a trusted and appreciated partner throughout Accenture’s projects, regardless of size or complexity. Whether the challenge is small or large, local or global, we approach our work with curiosity, care, and confidence. We foster an environment rooted in knowledge sharing, collaboration, and continuous learning, where people are encouraged to grow and succeed together.
   

  

 Use your knowledge and experience to collaborate closely with colleagues and leaders, contributing thoughtfully to innovation and helping shape sustainable, high‑quality solutions for our clients. Your work may include:
   

  

  

- Facilitating client workshops where we share knowledge and work together to explore, plan, and design solutions

- Designing, provisioning, and securing cloud architectures for both global and local business‑critical systems

- Supporting our clients by orchestrating and automating environments from proof‑of‑concept initiatives to production‑ready deployments

- Taking part in architecture and other learning initiatives, both as a learner and by sharing your expertise with others

 Throughout all of this, you will be part of an organization with strong ethical values one that takes pride in its work and places the well‑being and development of its people at the heart of everything it does.
   

  

 We believe: You are passionate about technology and its adoption. You have a strong background in IT infrastructure and are, or want to become, an expert within on-prem, cloud and hybrid cloud solutions, working with the major cloud providers such as Microsoft, AWS, Google and others.
   

  

 You are a team player with a focus on delivery with excellent communication and people skills.
   

  

 Here’s what you need:
   

  

  

- An academic degree, preferably at Master’s level or equivalent experience

- 2-5 years’ experience working with one or more cloud providers’ technologies such as AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, or on-prem technologies

- Experience with cloud ecosystems, from transforming requirements into design all the way down to operations

- Experience as a developer and/or technical architect

- Experience with infrastructure and thinks about automation in all stages of the construction and deployment processes

- The ability to articulate your knowledge to not only other technical people, but also to non-technical business focused people and help them understand what the technology would mean to them and why they should care

- Fluency in Swedish and English is a must, both spoken and written

 Bonus points if you have knowledge/experience in:
   

  

  

- Microservice architecture

- Design and scaling of IT systems

- CI/CD, automation and security

- Container platforms such as OpenShift, Google Anthos, Azure Kubernetes service, VMware Tanzu

- Integration architecture and integration/event solutions such as Cloud Suppliers Integration Services, Kafka, JBoss Fuse, Solace, Mule ESB

- API and Event driven architecture

- Technical architecture-standards, security and frameworks

- Infrastructure as Code (IaC), security controls, monitoring tools, DevOps-tools and services such as GitHub, Azure DevOps, Ant, Maven, Chef, Docker, Git, Jenkins

- Consulting or client service delivery

 How’s it like working for Accenture?  To give you a clearer impression of who we are and what we're looking for, we've asked Michael Nabb to tell us a bit about a career at Accenture:
   

  

*I’ve been with the firm for more than seventeen years and what has been consistent throughout those years has been a close collaboration with clients to help them achieve their goals. I’ve helped some of the largest businesses in the Nordics adapt to a changing world by introducing new technologies or improving the usage of existing ones. I look back and feel proud about playing an important part in the success of our clients.*
*Besides the delivery aspect Accenture has a very strong internal community with groups of interests as diverse as games, art, dance and all sorts of sports which I’ve personally enjoyed having participated in over the years.*
 What do we offer?
   

  

 Our dynamic offices offer a work environment where we enjoy spending time together whether through knowledge‑sharing evenings, breakfasts in the city, or social activities that strengthen our sense of community. With us, you have the opportunity to grow both professionally and personally, and we place strong emphasis on your development and well‑being.
   

  

 Our employees enjoy benefits such as a wellness allowance, health insurance, occupational pension, and enhanced parental leave.
   

  

 We also have our own sports association, Accenture IF, offering activities ranging from ball sports and climbing to sports trips and much more.
   

  

 Accenture’s internal women’s network, WISE (Women In Sweden), aims to engage and empower members while building a strong network across Accenture Sweden.
   

  

 You also have the opportunity to work in a hybrid or remote setup, depending on your assignments. We believe in maintaining a healthy work–life balance and making sure that life outside of work fits together smoothly. Flexible workload – you have control over your everyday life.
   

  

 Interested? If you have any questions regarding the role or the Software & Platform Engineering department, please contact Mathias Wiberg at mathias.wiberg@accenture.com . For questions regarding the position or the recruitment process please contact Lina Zangana, Lina.zangana@accenture.com.

title: Senior .NET utvecklare
description:
**Om rollen**
 Som Senior .NET-utvecklare blir du en nyckelperson i Skandias AML-team (Anti-Money Laundering), där ni utvecklar systemstöd för att förebygga penningtvätt och finansiering av terrorism – ett affärskritiskt uppdrag med tydlig samhällsnytta.
   

  

 Du arbetar nära både verksamhet och teknik och är delaktig i att designa, utveckla och vidareutveckla stabila och säkra lösningar. Rollen innebär att bidra i design-, arkitektur- och kravdiskussioner samt vara med och fatta tekniska vägval tillsammans med teamet.
   

  

 Arbetet fokuserar på backendutveckling i .NET/C# och eventdriven arkitektur i en säkerhetskänslig miljö med stora datamängder och höga krav på tillförlitlighet.
   

  

 Du utvecklar och optimerar integrationer mot andra system, samarbetar nära teamet genom exempelvis par- och mobbprogrammering och bidrar till kontinuerlig förbättring av kod, arbetssätt och lösningar.
   

  

**För att lyckas i rollen**
 Vi söker dig som trivs i en utvecklarroll där kvalitet, säkerhet och stabila lösningar är avgörande. Du är van att arbeta i affärskritiska systemmiljöer och har erfarenhet av att ta ansvar för tekniska lösningar genom hela utvecklingsprocessen – från design och implementation till vidareutveckling och förbättring.
   

  

 Du arbetar strukturerat, har ett analytiskt förhållningssätt och trivs med att samarbeta nära andra utvecklare. Samtidigt delar du gärna med dig av din kunskap och bidrar till att utveckla både teamets arbetssätt och tekniska lösningar.
   

  

 Du har även ett intresse för att ligga i framkant inom AI-stödd systemutveckling, till exempel genom verktyg som GitHub Copilot.
   

  

**Du har erfarenhet av**
* Systemutveckling i C# / .NET
* Backendutveckling och integrationer
* Eventdriven arkitektur eller meddelandebaserade lösningar
* ASP.NET / MVC och REST-baserade API:er
* Databashantering med MS SQL, samt ORM-verktyg som EF Core eller Dapper
* Versionshantering och CI/CD-flöden
* Kommunikation på svenska, i tal och skrift


**Vad händer nu?**
 Vi arbetar med löpande urval, så skicka gärna in din ansökan redan idag.
   

  

 Rekryteringsprocessen innehåller intervjuer, tester, bakgrundskontroll samt digital referenstagning för att säkerställa en rättvis och objektiv bedömning. Du kan läsa mer om vår process här.
   

  

 Om du har frågor kring rollen, vänligen kontakta rekryterande chef Stephan Mannerfelt på stephan.mannerfelt@skandia.se
   

  

 Om du har frågor om rekryteringsprocessen, vänligen kontakta Talent Acquisition Partner, Nina Fazeli på nina.fazeli@skandia.se
   

  

 Vi återkopplar till alla som söker, oavsett om du går vidare i processen eller inte.
   

  

**Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!**
**Mer om IT**
 Vi på Skandia IT är stolta över att vara en kundorienterad enhet som levererar effektiva och säkra lösningar för våra kunder. Med cirka 600 engagerade medarbetare är vi mitt i en spännande transformationsresa, där vi utvecklas tillsammans och har kul på vägen. Framåt tillsammans är vårt motto!
   

  

 Vi arbetar agilt och har sedan 2017 använt oss av SAFe (Scaled Agile Framework) ramverket, där leverans och kundvärde alltid står i centrum. Våra tågledningar och team, jobbar verksamhetsbaserat med aktiviteter som sprintplanering, daily stand-ups, team-synk, code reviews och retrospektiv.
   

  

 Var 12:e vecka samlas vi alla i vår ljusa och inspirerande Ljusgård på kontoret i Stockholm för 2 planeringsdagar. Planeringsperioden består av 6 tvåveckorssprintar, där den sista sprinten är en innovationssprint. Här får du chansen att, själv eller tillsammans med ditt team, utveckla och förfina våra system.

title: ITSM process owner
description:
Jobba för originalet.
   

  

 Välkommen till Apoteket, nummer ett sedan 1971. Med visioner för framtiden och kvalitet i varje detalj, skapar vi förutsättningar för ett liv i hälsa. Hos oss utvecklas du i en kultur där din röst alltid blir hörd. Och allt vi får över ger vi tillbaka till samhället. Det är så vi gör skillnad på riktigt.
   

  

  

**Om Digital infrastruktur, IT-säkerhet & Drift**
 På vår Tech-avdelning tror vi på att sätta människan i centrum – både våra kunder och våra medarbetare.
   

  

 I våra tvärfunktionella team ges alla möjlighet att ta initiativ och driva fram nya idéer, från koncept till produktion.
   

  

 Inom Digital infrastruktur, IT-säkerhet & drift arbetar vi för att säkerställa att Apotekets tekniska plattformar, driftmiljöer och arbetssätt håller hög kvalitet, är robusta och ständigt förbättras.
   

  

 Här samarbetar arkitekter, utvecklingsteam, säkerhetsfunktioner, helpdesk och driftleverantörer för att skapa en stabil, säker och förutsägbar teknisk grund för hela Apotekets verksamhet.
   

  

 I detta sammanhang spelar IT Service Management en avgörande roll. Tydliga processer, gemensamma arbetssätt och proaktiv styrning är avgörande för att vi ska kunna leverera stabila tjänster, hantera incidenter effektivt och förebygga störningar innan de uppstår.
   

  

  

**Din roll**
 Som ITSM process owner
   

  

 blir du en nyckelperson i Apotekets förmåga att arbeta strukturerat, enhetligt och proaktivt med ITIL-processer inom hela Apoteket Tech.
   

  

 Du har ett överordnat ansvar för våra ITIL-processer, framför allt incident, problem och change. Du äger processerna från definition till efterlevnad och förbättring – och säkerställer att de skapar verkligt värde i vardagen för både verksamhet och teknik.
   

  

 Rollen är både strategisk och operativ där du ansvarar för governance, arbetssätt och uppföljning och har samtidigt förmåga att vid behov ta en operativ ledande roll i incident- och problemhantering.
   

  

 Här kommer du in i en organisation med många beroenden och hög teknisk komplexitet, där målet är tydligt: färre incidenter, kortare ledtider och mer stabila leveranser. I rollen samarbetar du nära drift, helpdesk, säkerhet, utveckling och externa parter, med ett tydligt mandat att påverka och vidareutveckla hur Tech arbetar – både idag och framåt.
   

  

  

**Vad du kommer göra hos oss:**
* Äga, förvalta och vidareutveckla Apotekets ITIL-processer, främst incident, problem och change.
* Etablera gemensamma arbetssätt inom Helpdesk, Drift, Utveckling, Säkerhet och externa leverantörer.
* Säkerställa tydlig governance genom roller, ansvar (RACI), beslutsvägar och forum såsom CAB/Change-forum.
* Initiera och leda incident- och problemprocesser, utse incident manager per ärende samt driva rotorsaksanalyser och säkerställa att identifierade orsaker leder till konkreta åtgärder.
* Arbeta proaktivt med trendanalys, riskbedömning och förbättrade change-kontroller för att förebygga incidenter.
* Vara en aktiv kravställare mot utveckling och produkt baserat på insikter från drift, helpdesk och incidentdata.
* Införa och underhålla strukturerad förbättringscykel med KPI:er, uppföljning och efterlevnad.
* Ha en helhetsbild över processlandskapet – identifiera vad som behöver utvecklas, förenklas eller avvecklas.


**Vem söker vi?**
 Vi söker dig som är strukturerad, analytisk och trivs i en roll där du får kombinera processägarskap, teknisk förståelse och samarbete tvärfunktionellt i organisationen.
   

  

 Du är trygg i att ta ägarskap, fatta beslut och driva förändring – samtidigt som du är pragmatisk och fokuserad på att processer ska fungera i praktiken.
   

  

 Du gillar att ligga steget före, arbeta proaktivt, datadrivet och skapa ordning i komplexa miljöer. För dig är ITSM inte ett reaktivt stöd – utan ett verktyg för stabilitet, kvalitet och kontinuerlig förbättring.
   

  

  

**Vi ser att du:**
* Du har minst 5 års erfarenhet av ITIL/ITSM i större IT-miljöer.
* Du innehar ITIL-certifiering på Foundation-nivå (v3 eller senare).
* Du har arbetat i eller nära agila ramverk såsom Kanban/Scrum eller SAFe.
* Du har aktuell, praktisk erfarenhet av Incident och/eller Problem Management i moderna IT-miljöer.
* Du har erfarenhet av ha arbetat med governance, efterlevnad och processutveckling.
* Har mycket goda kunskaper i svenska, både i tal och skrift.


**Vi erbjuder...**
* Friskvårdsbidrag: 5000 kr per år.
* Personalrabatt: Förmånliga priser på många av våra produkter.
* Flexibla helgdagar: Möjlighet att byta arbetsfria helgdagar mot andra dagar på året.
* Förmånsportal (Benify): Erbjudanden inom bland annat kollektivtrafik, leasingbil, gym och hälsobehandlingar.
* Trygga försäkringar: Förstärkt föräldrapenning, tjänstepension (ITP) och kompletterande försäkringar.


**Om oss**
 Apoteket grundades 1971 och ägs av svenska staten, vilket innebär att vi arbetar både ansvarsfullt och långsiktigt. Vårt viktiga samhällsuppdrag handlar om att förbättra människors hälsa, läkemedelsanvändning och bidra till ökad patientsäkerhet. Med omkring 400 apotek och 3000 medarbetare över hela landet har vi nära samarbete med hälso- och sjukvården – alltid med omtanke om både människor och miljö.
   

  

 Vårt mål är tydligt: Ett liv i hälsa, för alla.
   

  

  

**Praktisk information:**
 Nyfiken? Det räcker gott.
   

  

 Hör av dig om rollen fångar ditt intresse! Vi berättar gärna mer om tjänsten och vår verksamhet – så du får en tydligare bild och kan avgöra om det känns helt rätt. Vänta bara inte för länge, urval och intervju sker löpande och tjänsten kan tillsättas innan sista ansökningsdatum.
   

  

 Skicka ditt CV tillsammans med svar på våra urvalsfrågor. Du behöver inte skriva ett personligt brev – frågorna är utformade för att hjälpa oss förstå mer om dina erfarenheter och kompetenser för matchning mot tjänst.
   

  

 Vi använder tester för objektivt urval som en del av vår rekryteringsprocess. Provanställning tillämpas.
   

  

 Välkommen till Apoteket!

title: Säkerhetschef till Nautrik
description:
Nautrik är ett av Sveriges snabbast växande defensetech bolag. Vi utvecklar avancerade autonoma marina system med fokus på autonomi, sensorfusion och samverkan mellan flera enheter och domäner.
   

  

 Våra lösningar är kostnadseffektiva, anpassningsbara och utvecklade för svenska och allierade försvarsmakter.
   

  

 Vi arbetar redan med svenska försvarsmyndigheter och deltar i nationella och internationella övningar. Idag är vi drygt 30 personer och växer snabbt.
   

  

 Nu söker vi en säkerhetschef som vill bygga vår säkerhetsfunktion från grunden.
   

  

**Rollen**
 Du ansvarar för att etablera och driva vårt säkerhetsarbete i takt med att bolaget skalar.
   

  

 Det här är en roll där du behöver kombinera struktur med tempo och kontinuerligt göra välavvägda beslut mellan affär, tillväxt och säkerhet.
   

  

 Vi söker dig med hög integritet, som är trygg i att fatta egna beslut och stå för dem, även när det innebär att säga nej.
   

  

**Ansvar:**
* Bygga och utveckla säkerhetsskyddsorganisation
* Ta fram styrdokument, processer och rutiner
* Säkerställa efterlevnad av säkerhetsskyddslagen (2018:585)
* Ansvara för personal-, informations- och fysisk säkerhet
* Arbeta med skalskydd, passersystem och larm
* Genomföra risk- och sårbarhetsanalyser samt driva åtgärdsarbete
* Utveckla incidenthantering
* Utbilda och stödja organisationen i säkerhetsfrågor
* Vara kontaktpunkt gentemot myndigheter


**Vi tror att du:**
* Har minst 5 års erfarenhet av säkerhetsarbete
* Har erfarenhet av säkerhetsskyddsarbete i säkerhetskänslig verksamhet
* Har god kunskap om säkerhetsskyddslagen (2018:585)
* Har bakgrund från försvar, myndighet eller liknande (meriterande)
* Har erfarenhet av personal-, fysisk- eller IT-säkerhet
* Är strukturerad, analytisk och självgående
* Är kommunikativ och pedagogisk
* Relevant utbildning inom säkerhet/risk, YH, officers- eller polisexamen eller motsvarande erfarenhet är meriterande.


**Varför Nautrik?**
* Bygg säkerhetsfunktionen från grunden
* Arbeta nära ledning och militärteknik i framkant
* Stor påverkan på både struktur, kultur och produkt
* Vara med på en snabb tillväxtresa inom defensetech
* Hjälpa till att bygga det svenska försvaret


**Praktiskt:**
* Heltid, tillsvidare (6 mån provanställning)
* Svenskt medborgarskap krävs
* Säkerhetsprövning genomförs
* Stationerad i Stockholm och förväntad närvaro på kontoret

title: Field Service Engineer - X-ray (Stockholm)
description:
**Job Description Summary**
 You will be part of a successful and dedicated service team. In this role, you will be responsible for performing the daily service on our customer's imaging equipment within the x-ray segment – mammography, conventional x-ray, vascular and surgery equipment. You will work independently and are expected to develop and maintain strong relationships with our customers.
   

  

 We are now looking to fill one position with main location in Stockholm.
   

  

 GE HealthCare is a leading global medical technology and digital solutions innovator. Our purpose is to create a world where healthcare has no limits. Unlock your ambition, turn ideas into world-changing realities, and join an organization where every voice makes a difference, and every difference builds a healthier world.
   

  

**Job Description**
**Main responsibilities**
 As a Field Service Engineer, you will perform scheduled maintenance and corrective service at our customer's sites. You will get the opportunity to work with cutting edge technology, performing service on medical equipment and be a part of a company where we’re building a healthier future and create a world where healthcare has no limits. In your role on the field, you will meet with our customers almost daily and build close customer relationships. You will work in a company where you will be challenged to be better every day, and where you will be encouraged to learn and grow.
   

  

 Based out of your home or our office in Stockholm, you are responsible for planning all travel and admin for an effective workday including visiting customer sites, document performed service, administrate return of spare parts and other task related to the performed service. You will have main responsibility for customer sites in the area you’re based but must be prepared to travel and stay overnight for work on sites further from your main location, sometimes on a short notice.
   

  

**We are looking for a** 
**Field Service Engineer** 
**that** 
**meet** 
**s many** 
**of the following requirements**
* Class B driver's license.
* EU work permit.
* Commercial understanding.
* Is accountable for own work.
* Positive attitude, good sense of humour and team spirit.
* Experience from the industry and a similar position.
* Knowledge of IT, Linux/Microsoft.
* Willing to travel extensively in the job.
* Want to establish and maintain good customer relationships.
* Detail oriented and structured.
* Can work independently.
* Is flexible, ambitious and have a good overview.


**GE Health** 
**C** 
**are offers you**
* A thorough introduction to the equipment you need to service together with skilled colleagues and at GE Healthcare's training centres.
* Dedicated colleagues willing to help and support you.
* Opportunity to work with cutting edge technology.
* Professional and personal development within an international organization.
* Flexible car policy.
* Possibility to work from home or from offices in Gothenburg or Stockholm.
* Eligible for overtime.


**Inclusion and Diversity**
 GE HealthCare is an Equal Opportunity Employer where inclusion matters. Employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion, national or ethnic origin, sex, sexual orientation, g ender identity or expression, age, disability, protected veteran status or other characteristics protected by law.
   

  

**Behaviors**
 We expect all employees to live and breathe our behaviors: to act with humility and build trust; lead with transparency; deliver with focus, and drive ownership – always with unyielding integrity.
   

  

**Total Rewards**
 Our total rewards are designed to unlock your ambition by giving you the boost and flexibility you need to turn your ideas into world-changing realities. Our salary and benefits are everything you’d expect from an organization with global strength and scale, and you’ll be surrounded by career opportunities in a culture that fosters care, collaboration and support.
   

  

******Additional Information******
**Relocation Assistance Provided:** 
 No

title: Delivery Excellence Lead – Tieto Caretech (m/f/d)
description:
**Job Description**
**At Tieto Caretech you’ll get a chance to provide citizens and end users with product offerings that help them in their life, reinventing the world for good. If that sounds interesting, please keep on reading.**
**About Us**
 Through advanced, intuitive technology, Tieto Caretech reduces administrative work, improves decision-making, and strengthens safety and quality in health and social care. With the Lifecare software portfolio we enable health and social care professionals to focus on what matters most: people.
   

  

 We are a global team of professionals working on providing products and services to different sectors from intensive care to laboratory and social care. In addition to working closely with our customers, we also have our own products that we build and develop for various fields in society. From enabling healthcare professionals to spend more valuable time with their patients to providing data for professional athletes to achieve even better results, we make all that possible. With us you can be part of advancing societies through purposeful technology and make your mark on the health and wellbeing of people.
   

  

**About The Role**
 Do you want to work with a market-leading and modern product portfolio designed for the next generation of health and welfare solutions? Do you have experience in program and project management and a strong understanding of healthcare and welfare services? Do you enjoy working closely with customers to ensure successful deliveries and implementations?
   

  

 The Delivery Excellence Lead will be responsible for innovating, establishing and managing the PMO function, implementing project management best practices, and overseeing the execution of multiple healthcare software projects. This role requires strong leadership, strategic and tactical thinking, and a deep understanding of modern software development methodologies and healthcare industry standards.
   

  

**In this role you will**
* Develop and implement Project Management Office (PMO) frameworks, standards, and methodologies tailored to the healthcare industry/domain.
* Establish governance needed for project implementations and steering group meetings.
* Ensure alignment of projects with organizational strategy and regulatory requirements.
* Monitor project performance, risks, and issues, and provide actionable insights.
* Facilitate communication between project teams and senior leadership.
* Provide mentorship and guidance to project managers and ensure project objectives e.g. cost, quality and schedule are met.
* Promote continuous improvement within the area.
* Ensuring the project and reporting tools are reflecting the delivery/project statuses.


**We are looking for someone with**
* Bachelor’s or master’s degree in computer science, business, or healthcare-related field
* 10+ years of experience in project/program management within a software or healthcare IT environment.
* Proven experience in establishing and managing project setups and budgeting.
* Experience in managing end-to-end programs, deliveries, and projects, preferably within IT solutions, health & care, or the public sector.
* Excellent leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills.
* Structured and proactive mindset with the ability to oversee multiple projects simultaneously.
* Fluency in English, both written and spoken, is required. Knowledge of Finnish, Swedish, or Norwegian is an advantage.
* Proficiency in project management tools.


 As a person, you are an engaged and open team player who enjoys working with diverse cultures, driving projects forward, and ensuring high-quality deliveries. You are self-motivated, solution-oriented, and thrive in a role that combines both independent work and collaboration with colleagues and customers.
   

  

**What’s in it for you?**
 With us, you will be part of important and meaningful work in the healthcare and welfare sector. You have an important mission to make a difference that really matters. In addition, as a company we offer you professional growth, open and friendly culture, and an outstanding work-life balance! We believe that our organizational culture is an important part of enabling you to be successful. We provide a flexible hybrid work model as part of our culture and way of working.
   

  

 Finally, we also believe in curiosity and learning as a lifestyle. We want to encourage you to keep up that curiosity and deepen your knowledge on topics you’re interested in. For example, we host a ‘Keep Learning Week’ twice a year, a week where everyone at Tieto has the possibility to join different training courses in a truly global setting.
   

  

**Could you be our new colleague?**
 We look forward to hearing from you! We will fill the position as soon as we find the right person, so we encourage you to apply today. If you have any questions regarding this role, please contact the hiring managers Mubashsharaha.Khan@tieto.com and Tommi.Vigman@tieto.com
   

  

 We perform background checks on all final candidates.
   

  

 Tieto declines calls from recruitment companies.
   

  

 Additional Information
   

  

 At Tieto, we believe in the power of diversity, equity, and inclusion. We encourage applicants of all backgrounds, genders (m/f/d), and walks of life to join our team, as we believe that this fosters an inspiring workplace and fuels innovation. Our commitment to openness, trust, and diversity is at the heart of our mission to create digital futures that benefit businesses, societies, and humanity.
   

  

 Diversity, equity and inclusion (tietoevry.com)

title: Business Controller
description:
**Want to shape the future of waterborne transport and join a world-class team?**
 Candela is the
 **global leader in electric hydrofoil vessels** 
 . Since 2014, we’ve gone from building our first premium electric speedboat in a small Stockholm workshop to delivering game-changing vessels around the globe.
 **The Candela P-12** 
 is our latest innovation - the world’s first electric hydrofoil ferry is not just operating in Stockholm, but now attracting orders from around the world.
   

  

 Our boats “fly” thanks to
 **computer-guided** 
 underwater wings under the hull made out of
 **lightweight carbon fiber.** 
 The wings lift the vessel above the water, which results in cutting energy use by up to 80%, while offering zero emissions and a far better passenger experience than traditional diesel vessels.
   

  

 And that’s exactly why we do this. Our mission is clear: to
 **speed up the transition to fossil fuel–free lakes and oceans** 
 .
   

  

 You can be part of the amazing team in Candela now! We are looking for
 **Business Controller** 
 to support Candela’s future growth ambitions.
   

  

**Purpose and Goals**
 As a Business Controller, you work across the areas of financial and operational planning and analysis as well as with KPI and financial reporting. Supporting the strategic planning for Candela is also part of the role. You have a strong contribution in developing new ways of (digital) working within our Finance function as we scale operations. This is a high-impact role for someone who wants to shape the financial direction of a fast-scaling technology company, with exposure to investor reporting and board-level planning.
   

  

**Your core responsibilities will include:**
* Drive and develop the financial planning process, including quarterly forecasting, scenario analysis, and business planning in support of capital raises.
* Compile operational (incl. production) and financial information into monthly reports for management and the board of directors.
* Support ad-hoc reporting, special projects including financial and operational modelling.
* Drive digitalization, use of BI systems and automation of workflows within our controlling / finance function.


**Qualifications**
* Bachelor’s degree within Business Administration, preferably with focus on accounting.
* +3 years working experience with accounting and or controlling.
* Effective communication skills in English, both verbal and written.
* Analytical mind and passion for numbers driven analysis.


**Form of employment** 
 : Full-time with initial 6 month probation period
   

  

**Start date:** 
 ASAP
   

  

**Location/base** 
 : Rotebro/Frihamnen
   

  

**Team** 
 : Finance
   

  

**Reporting to** 
 : Finance Director
   

  

**Why people love working at Candela?**
 At Candela, we’re not just building electric vessels - we’re creating a whole new category of clean, efficient waterborne transport. You get the chance to work with
 **cutting-edge technology** 
 , solving complex challenges, and seeing the results in action - sometimes literally, when we organize test rides for our newcomers! We’re a diverse and ambitious team of over 50 nationalities, united by a
 **revolutionary spirit and kindness** 
 . That means we work hard, but always with humility and while having fun - whether it’s solving a manufacturing challenge, improving processes, or cheering each other on during
 **Candela Months** 
 , our year-round calendar of bonding activities and events.
   

  

 We’re
 **office-first but not office-only** 
 . We believe collaboration is most powerful when we build together, in person - but we also trust our team to work in ways that fit their life. At Candela, you’ll find a place where bold ideas are welcomed, great people are celebrated, and the ride is just as important as the destination.
   

  

**Recruitment process:**
 We aim to keep our recruitment process as straightforward and efficient as possible. As an international company with English as our corporate language, the entire process is conducted in English. Therefore, please submit your application in our corporate language as well. Kindly note that we cannot process applications sent via email due to GDPR so please use our career website for you application.
   

  

**We welcome the opportunity to meet you!**

title: Customer Success Partner Senior Advisor, Sweden
description:
**We help the world run better**
 At SAP, we keep it simple: you bring your best to us, and we'll bring out the best in you. We're builders touching over 20 industries and 80% of global commerce, and we need your unique talents to help shape what's next. The work is challenging – but it matters. You'll find a place where you can be yourself, prioritize your wellbeing, and truly belong. What's in it for you? Constant learning, skill growth, great benefits, and a team that wants you to grow and succeed.
   

  

**Summary**
 SAP Business Data Cloud is a technology platform in Data & AI solution area that brings together data and analytics, artificial intelligence & intelligent applications in one, unified environment. SAP Business Data Cloud is one of the highest profiles and fastest growing parts of the SAP portfolio and critical to SAP helping our customers innovate and grow with SAP. In EMEA alone BTP-BDC represents a 1BN Euro business supporting > 8,000 customers. Aside from being the foundation for SAP applications it provides a modern platform of cloud capabilities to enables customers to extend, enhance and innovate above and beyond their existing applications.
   

  

 The focus of this position is to join the SAP BDC Customer Success Manager (Customer success solution advisor) team and become the trusted advisor to some of our flagship EMEA customers, helping them drive a roadmap for SAP BDC adoption and consumption across their organization.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* You will proactively partner with customers to sustainably increase consumption of SAP BDC & Analytical services, and coordinate activities with the Account Team, by building out a joint outcome-based Adoption plan and drive a mutual understanding of IT-Architecture and Business value.
* You understand the big picture, deliver expertise with digital transformation across different lines of business, advise on technical and business architectures.
* You are the primary SAP BDC strategic point of contact and will orchestrate success resources across SAP and customers, leveraging assets of the SAP BDC engagement model.
* You are the primary point of contact to drive customer transformation journey strategy towards SAP Business Data Cloud
* You will analyze your customer’s Vision, Technical needs and execute consumption plans for your assigned customers.
* You will instruct and prepare the customer to make the best use of the solution, in alignment with SAP strategy and roadmap.
* You will be a voice of the customer to internal SAP stakeholders. You will work with colleagues from a broad variety of groups such as Cloud Success Services life-cycle relevant centers of experts, Account Teams, Pre-sales, Product Management groups, Support, Services, Partners, etc.
* BDC -CSP Senior Advisor may also be assigned to fulfill a customer’s premium subscription services.


**Role Requirements**
 You will excel in this role if you:
   

  

* Have 5-6 years of experience working in the IT Industry in Customer facing roles. Your background could be Software Engineering, Solution Advisory & Management, Project/Product Management, Project Implementation, Pre-sales, Technical Customer Engagement, Customer Success, Consulting, etc.
* Have excellent understanding of one or more areas of the Data & Analytics portfolio - SAP Business Data Cloud covering Datasphere , SAP Analytics Cloud, Intelligent applications , BW- BW4HANA , Databricks ML – covering primarily Reporting , Planning and forecasting solutions.
* Have a working knowledge of hybrid and cloud-based landscapes and can guide customers on Solution Advisory , Cloud Strategies and Architecture guidance.
* Certifications in SAP Business Data cloud solutions – Datasphere , SAP Analytics cloud , BW4HANA – BW , Enterprise architecture are a plus.
* Have foundational knowledge of public cloud platforms and providers (e.g. Microsoft , Annaplan , Snowflake etc.)
* Have professional experience with implementing cloud initiatives.
* Have excellent communication and presentation skills. Familiarity with design thinking methodology is a plus.
* Communicate proactively and can prioritize efficiently. You have a strong sense of accountability.
* Can manage complex organizational dynamics both internally at SAP, as well as externally with customers and partners.
* Can continuously learn and adapt to the unique business and cultural practices of each region/country.
* Have a strong customer service mindset and customer empathy.
* Have a passion for learning new technologies and enjoy problem solving.
* Thrive in a dynamic team-based environment and can guide new team members into the role.


**Other Requirements**
* Fluent in both English and local language Swedish or Danish or Finnish
* Already situated in Sweden as relocation is not supported for this position


**Location**
 Stockholm or Gothenburg
   

  

**Our Team**
 We pride on a work environment that is inclusive and diverse. Every idea and perspective are valued.
   

  

 The SAP Business Data Cloud CSP team is committed to sustain each other to ensure success, both individually and as a team. We have a pay it forward mentality and are always willing to help our colleagues. Our culture is built upon sharing best practices, utilizing communication platforms to answer each other’s questions, and bringing an entrepreneurial
   

  

 spirit to the mission of driving SAP BDC consumption growth together. We provide opportunities to lead regional and global workstreams that help us improve as a team and contribute to great customer experiences
   

  

**Bring out your best**
 SAP innovations help more than four hundred thousand customers worldwide work together more efficiently and use business insight more effectively. Originally known for leadership in enterprise resource planning (ERP) software, SAP has evolved to become a market leader in end-to-end business application software and related services for database, analytics, intelligent technologies, and experience management. As a cloud company with two hundred million users and more than one hundred thousand employees worldwide, we are purpose-driven and future-focused, with a highly collaborative team ethic and commitment to personal development. Whether connecting global industries, people, or platforms, we help ensure every challenge gets the solution it deserves. At SAP, you can bring out your best.
   

  

**We win with inclusion**
 SAP’s culture of inclusion, focus on health and well-being, and flexible working models help ensure that everyone – regardless of background – feels included and can run at their best. At SAP, we believe we are made stronger by the unique capabilities and qualities that each person brings to our company, and we invest in our employees to inspire confidence and help everyone realize their full potential. We ultimately believe in unleashing all talent and creating a better world.
   

  

 SAP is committed to the values of Equal Employment Opportunity and provides accessibility accommodations to applicants with physical and/or mental disabilities. If you are interested in applying for employment with SAP and are in need of accommodation or special assistance to navigate our website or to complete your application, please send an e-mail with your request to Recruiting Operations Team: Careers@sap.com.
   

  

 For SAP employees: Only permanent roles are eligible for the SAP Employee Referral Program, according to the eligibility rules set in the SAP Referral Policy. Specific conditions may apply for roles in Vocational Training.
   

  

 Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to their age, race, religion, national origin, ethnicity, gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, et al), sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or disability, in compliance with applicable federal, state, and local legal requirements.
   

  

 Successful candidates might be required to undergo a background verification with an external vendor.
   

  

**AI Usage in the Recruitment Process**
 For information on the responsible use of AI in our recruitment process, please refer to our Guidelines for Ethical Usage of AI in the Recruiting Process.
   

  

 Please note that any violation of these guidelines may result in disqualification from the hiring process.

title: Logistics Co-Founder / CTO (100 % remote) (m/f/d)
description:
We are looking to hire ambitious entrepreneurs to start and scale their own startups.
   

  

 We are serial entrepreneurs, for example Paul Müller (founder Adjust, €1.2B exit) and Petter Made (founder SumUp, €8B) who are eager to support outlier personalities and serial entrepreneurs to build €1B+ companies.
   

  

 Our offer:
   

  

* A salary while you build your startup as you will directly be employed by us. Alternatively, you can opt for up to €500k in funding.
* 1:1 sparring with unicorn founders on a weekly basis
* Community: Access to the top 0.1% of founders, peers and investors
* Team building: Hiring top notch talent supported through our network (over 50,000 professionals)
* Distribution: Support in reaching product-market-fit and building up a sales force / marketing machine
* Funding support for securing a multi-million euro funding round within 12 months (on average, EWOR Fellows raise > €2M after our Grand Pitch)


 One of our fellows set a record for Europe’s largest pre-seed round by a first-time founder, securing a €12M pre-seed investment.
   

  

 Tasks
   

  

* You will own, build, and run your startup in fields such as Logistics
* You will embark on an extensive personal development journey crafted by unicorn founders and follow a fully customised programme enhancing your goal, time, and energy management
* You will receive support in hiring through our network to over 50,000 professionals and advice as well as best practices from serial entrepreneurs
* You will receive intensive coaching to make your startup ready to raise millions in funding
* You will iterate your product with us until having reached product-market-fit and receive support in building up a sales force or creating a marketing engine respectively


**Requirements**
* You are based in Europe or the Americas or open to relocate
* You are willing to take full responsibility for your own startup and scale it to €100M+ in revenues
* You have excellent communication skills in the English language

title: Technical Compliance Specialist
description:
**Description**
*Position at LeoVegas Group*
**About The Role**
 As a Technical Compliance Specialist you’ll join a team of all rounders from various backgrounds who share a unique passion for technology and compliance. You’ll work across a variety of products and jurisdictions, helping to ensure compliance with the applicable laws, regulations and guidelines.
   

  

 Reporting to the Senior Technical Compliance Specialist, you will work with teams across the business and be exposed to requirements spanning the iGaming business.
   

  

 YOU WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR
   

  

* Delivering regulatory requirements to the Product and Tech organisation and review designs against compliance;
* Mastering product compliance requirements for regulated markets, ensure that all the internal tools are up to date with the latest applicable version of the requirements and provide interpretation and guidance as appropriate;
* Contributing to promoting a compliance culture within the organisation;
* Being highly involved in technical audit activities;
* Supporting and closely cooperating with the wider legal and compliance teams to address findings and incidents,
* Contributing to improving and consolidating the Compliance posture of the Group;
* Supporting the internal teams with replies to technical compliance queries;
* Monitoring and assessing changing requirements in new and existing markets and ensuring timely analysis and announcements.


 OUR SUCCESSFUL CANDIDATE WILL HAVE
   

  

* At least 2 years of relevant experience in the compliance or product technology space in the online gambling industry.
* Understanding of remote gambling legislation
* Firm grasp of the igaming industry and the role of compliance in building technical requirements
* Nice to have skills:
* Understanding of the Agile methodology, project management or change management.
* Previous experience with Atlassian suite


**Who We Are**
 At the core of LeoVegas Group, is Team Leo. Our culture is our foundation and is what enables us to innovate, build, and lead as we trailblaze our way through the igaming industry. We’re a team of over 2000 innovators, initiators, and groundbreakers working in a fast-paced and agile environment across 19 offices world-wide.
   

  

**Benefits**
* Hybrid work policy
* 4 weeks of Workation (T&C apply)
* 30 annual vacation days
* Occupational Pension
* 5,000 SEK wellness contribution annually
* Parental Leave Top-Up
* Possibility to enrol in a private health care insurance for both you and your partner
* 1,500 SEK equipment allowance
* Benify - benefits portal with many offers and discounts


 JOIN US!
   

  

 In our pride, we like to empower our teammates to find their roar and run with their wildest ideas. We don’t wait around for things to happen, we pounce and make it happen!
   

  

 If you think you would be a good fit for the Leo pride - give us a roar!
   

  

 \*\*As our company working language is English we’d like to see your CV in English please\*\*

title: Senior Enterprise Architect
description:
As of 2 September 2025, Vivicta, former Tietoevry Tech Services, now operates as a standalone company under our new brand. We’ve officially launched Vivicta, your trusted Nordic transformation partner, making everyday easier through technology. You can explore more about our brand and what we stand for at https://www.vivicta.com
   

  

 Ready to join us? Click ‘Apply’ and complete our short application form to get started. Prefer another option? Apply via LinkedIn to automatically populate your details using your profile.
   

  

**Senior Enterprise Architect – Vivicta**
 We seek a experienced Enterprise Architect to join Vivicta, a multi-cloud platform provider offering scalable infrastructure and advanced solutions for software development, data, and AI. In this role, you will translate business needs into effective architectures and technologies, ensuring long-term value in our solutions.
   

  

**When joining Vivicta as a Senior Enterprise Architect** 
 , you will:
   

  

* Help Vivicta by developing architectures and technology approaches that meet the business needs and customer demands, helping to drive forward customer satisfaction and business growth
* Ensure that architectural designs align with business requirements and support organisational objectives
* Oversee the architecture to comprehensively address all dimensions of the business, including people, processes, technology, information, systems, and necessary changes
* Define and implement architectures in accordance with recognised design practices, principles, standards, and policies
* Develop and validate architectures and system high-level designs
* Verify the conceptual integrity of technical solutions
* Incorporate appropriate security and compliance considerations into architectural design
* Maintain adherence to best practices for governance, regulatory compliance, and sustainable investment throughout solution development
* Ensure that technology strategies are consistently applied and effectively support the business strategy, as well as key initiatives and projects
* Contribute actively to Vivicta Enterprise Architecture.


**Who Are We Looking For**
* Demonstrates strong motivation and collaboration skills to support the success of Vivicta’s business.
* Makes decisions based on factual information and accepts accountability for outcomes.
* Strives for architectural excellence and delivers high satisfaction to the services
* Possesses relevant architectural knowledge and/or certifications such as TOGAF, IASA, or equivalent.
* Is fluent in both spoken and written English.
* Excels at creating clear and visually compelling presentations that simplify complex issues for diverse stakeholders.
* Exhibits outstanding communication abilities with the capacity to inspire and drive change.
* Experience in a similar role within the IT industry is preferred.
* Holds a suitable academic degree.


 You communicate well, collaborate effectively with colleagues and partners, and are eager to learn. You work in a structured manner and manage complex architectures involving many stakeholders.
   

  

**We Offer**
 We offer you professional growth, meaningful projects, open culture and an outstanding work-life balance! We believe that our organizational culture is an important part of enabling you to be successful. We provide a flexible hybrid work model as part of our culture and way of working. Finally, we also believe in curiosity and learning as a lifestyle where you need to unlearn and relearn every day as new possibilities emerge in the rapidly changing world of technology and technology services..
   

  

**Did we get you inspired?**
 We look forward to your application! Please attach your CV or LinkedIn-profile and we’ll reach out to you. Interviews will be held on a continuous basis, so we encourage you to apply today. If you have any questions regarding this role, please contact Mikko Sellgren (mikko.sellgren@vivicta.com, Tel +358504867361)
   

  

 Hope to hear from you soon!
   

  

 Location: Finland/Sweden/Norway/Czechia
   

  

*We perform background checks on all final candidates.*
*Vivicta declines calls from recruitment companies.*

title: Client & Consulting Executive
description:
B2C | COPENHAGEN / AARHUS
   

  

**Do you dream of shaping the future for leading consumer brands? At IMPACT, we’re seeking to strengthen our B2C/Retail Client & Consulting muscles to drive transformative growth for our clients, act as a trusted advisor and strategic partner.**
**About The Job**
 This is your unique opportunity to play a central role in defining digital roadmaps, nurturing high-level relationships, and steering complex programs with purpose and clarity. You will own client relationships and lead the charge on consulting engagements, while partnering with C-level leaders (CDOs, CMOs, CIOs, etc.), identifying new growth opportunities and ensuring project success and stakeholder alignment. Working hand-in-hand with our delivery teams, you will help clients unlock the power of digital to meet their ambitions - today as well as into the future.
   

  

 The position at IMPACT is for you, who thrives to
   

  

* Turn ambition into action by designing bold digital strategies and roadmaps that truly move the needle for our clients.
* Lead the charge on high-impact consulting engagements in the fast-evolving worlds of retail, D2C, and omnichannel.
* Fuel growth by spotting and seizing opportunities to expand client partnerships through smart cross- and upselling initiatives.
* Be the trusted voice that digital leaders turn to guiding C-level decision-makers and middle managers alike with confidence and clarity.
* Inspire and align teams by orchestrating complex projects, delegating with purpose, and creating a collaborative, energized environment.
* Pitch ideas that win, shaping standout business proposals that captivate clients and deliver measurable results.
* Own the outcome driving satisfaction, retention, and performance across a portfolio of clients generating €1–5M+ annually.


 YOUR PROFILE
   

  

 You are experienced within leading complex digital commerce transformations, driving strategic consulting across retail, D2C, and omnichannel landscapes, while building strong, trust-based client relationships.
   

  

 Thus, we expect you to demonstrate
   

  

* A strategic mindset with strong communication and analytical skills
* A natural connector who builds trust with clients and teams alike
* Experience with omnichannel, loyalty, personalization, and digital transformation
* Proven ability to drive business development and innovation


 We imagine you have 5-8 years' experience in management consulting or project/program leadership with a strong focus on Commerce. Additionally, you could have an educational background within IT, business, finance, or management (BSc or MSc).
   

  

 Finally, we are looking for a colleague and team member who can contribute to a fun and professional work environment at IMPACT while ensuring a high standard in collaboration with your colleagues.
   

  

 LIFE AT IMPACT
   

  

 For more than 20 years, IMPACT has pioneered some of the most advanced commerce solutions in the Nordics. What started in Aarhus, Denmark has grown into a European powerhouse with offices across the world.
   

  

 While we cover all the major tech platforms and work with some of the most exciting businesses, we make a sincere effort to stay down-to-earth. It’s who we are, and it’s the kind of talent we want to attract. We empower people and businesses to drive positive change and quality experiences, and we’re not afraid to take the lead on shaping the future of commerce.
   

  

 Today, we’re a global team of digital talents working to strategize, craft, and support all things tech. We push the boundaries for commerce with curiosity, ambition, and empowerment that cements the ambition to
 **change commerce for the better** 
 . But it’s not all about size. Because more than anything, we want to maintain our collective can-do attitude that creates a working environment where everything is possible – and have fun while doing it.
   

  

 CONTACT
   

  

 Sound like an opportunity for you? Send your CV via the link below.
   

  

 Interviews are held on an ongoing basis, as the position is to be filled as soon as possible.
   

  

 If you have any questions, you are welcome to contact Executive Search Specialist Christian Kerrn-Jespersen +45 23299249.
   

  

 We are looking forward to hearing from you!
   

  

 For more than 20 years, IMPACT has pioneered some of the most advanced commerce solutions in the Nordics. What started in Aarhus, Denmark has grown into a European powerhouse with offices across the world.
   

  

 Today, we’re a global team of digital talents working to strategize, craft, and support all things tech. We push the boundaries for commerce with curiosity, ambition, and empowerment that cements the ambition to
 **change commerce for the better**
