 . But it’s not all about size. Because more than anything, we want to maintain our collective can-do attitude that creates a working environment where everything is possible – and have fun while doing it.

title: Infrastrukturarkitekt Centrala Plattformar
description:
Vill du arbeta med några av Handelsbankens mest affärskritiska system och vara med och säkra driften på en av bankens mest betydelsefulla plattformar? Vi söker en Infrastrukturarkitekt med erfarenhet av IBM Z och z/OS som trivs i komplexa miljöer med stora transaktionsvolymer och höga krav på säkerhet.
   

  

**Ditt team**
 Du blir en del av Centrala Plattformar, där cirka 45 medarbetare arbetar i fyra team placerade i Stockholm, Göteborg och Örebro. Centrala Plattformar har ansvaret för två av bankens mest centrala plattformar – System IBM z och IBM i – där många av Handelsbankens mest transaktionskritiska system körs, såsom kärnsystem för:
   

  

* Internetbank och mobil
* Kontorssystem
* Korttjänster
* Inlåning
* Stora delar av bankens internationella banksystem


 Plattformarna är en kritisk del av bankens totala tekniska infrastruktur. IBM Z är en strategisk plattform på Handelsbanken som skall vara en del i bankens hybrida IT landskap som är en blandning av Azure cloud, on premises cloud-teknologi, samt kärnsystem på IBM Z, varför integrationsförmågor är nyckeln till vår framtid.
   

  

 Arbetet sker enligt agila principer, och teamet deltar i SAFe‑liknande ceremonier, inklusive PI‑planering.
   

  

**Din roll**
 Detta är en renodlad arkitektroll som proaktivt driver arkitekturarbete inom sitt ansvarsområde i enlighet med Handelsbankens strategier och målarkitekturer. I rollen förväntas man röra sig mellan verksamhet och teknik samt mellan helhet och detalj - viktiga egenskaper för att kunna hantera en modern, intelligent och hybrid infrastruktur.
   

  

 Rollen koordinerar och verkar för IT-strategisk förflyttning, sprider kunskap och stöttar team i lösnings- och arkitekturarbete.
   

  

 Infrastrukturarkitektens huvudsakliga arbetsuppgifter:
   

  

* Delta i analyser kring förbättringar av plattformen
* Informera intressenter om progressen
* Dokumentation (spårbarhet, nuläge, lösningsförslag beslutsunderlag)
* Ägandeskap av arkitekturen och road map
* Regelefterlevnad


 Som Arkitekt är du ansvarig för high level design och varje kompetensområde har en techlead som är ansvariga för low level design och som arbetar nära arkitekterna, så arkitekten beskriver VAD som ska åstadkommas men techlead definierar exakt HUR det tekniskt ska implementeras.
   

  

 Som Arkitekt på mainframe-området så samarbetar du även med arkitekter på övriga plattformsområden med regelbundna möten för gemensamt ställningstagande runt tekniska frågeställningar och informationsspridning mellan plattformarna.
   

  

**Vad rollen erbjuder**
* Arbete i en tekniskt avancerad och affärskritisk mijö, där din specialistkompetens gör stor skillnad.
* Möjlighet att arbeta nära både drift, utveckling och arkitektur.
* Samt nedan förmåner:


**Din profil**
 Vi söker dig som tidigare arbetat i en Tech Lead-roll eller har tidigare arkitekterfarenhet. Du har god kunskap om IBM Z (stordator/mainframe) och z/OS. Du trivs med att arbeta i komplexa systemmiljöer med stora transaktionsvolymer och höga krav på säkerhet och tillgänglighet. Du är trygg och säker i det svenska och engelska språket, både i tal och skrift.
   

  

 Meriterande är erfarenhet av att arbeta i miljöer med sekventiella arbetsflöden (tågliknande), där ändringar i ett system kan påverka andra delar av processen.
   

  

 Utöver ovan är det meriterande om du:
   

  

* Har en bredare allmän IT kompetens (utanför IBM Z)
* Har viss erfarenhet av Linux server (oavsett arkitektur)
* Har kunskaper om datakommunikation


 Även om teknisk kompetens är viktigt, lägger vi stor vikt vid dina personliga egenskaper och social kompetens.
   

  

 Vi söker dig som har en god kommunikationsförmåga och tycker om att samarbeta med olika personer och intressenter för att nå gemensamma mål. Du är strukturerad och trivs med att ta ansvar för att planera och driva arkitekturarbete framåt.
   

  

 Din analytiska förmåga gör att du gärna tar fram tydliga dokument och underlag som hjälper teamet att fatta bra beslut. Du är också nyfiken på teknik och engagerar dig i att utveckla plattformen på smarta och effektiva sätt.
   

  

**Välkommen med din ansökan!**
*Som ett steg i Handelsbankens rekryteringsprocess krävs en godkänd bakgrundskontroll på slutkandidat, utförd av en extern part, innan anställning kan ske.*
**Kontaktuppgifter**
**Rekryterande chef:** 
 Elin Gräslund, elin.graslund@handelsbanken.se
   

  

**Talent Acquisition Partner:** 
 Henrik Alm, henrik.alm2@handelsbanken.se
   

  

**Facklig representant Finansförbundet:** 
 Per Almshult, per.almshult@handelsbanken.se
   

  

**Facklig representant Akademikerföreningen:** 
 Bjarne Nilsson, bjarne.nilsson@handelsbanken.se

title: Client Strategy Consultant
description:
At Upsales, we’re not “another CRM vendor.” We’re building Europe’s most advanced AI-driven revenue platform. And as more ambitious B2B companies rely on us to drive predictable growth, we need someone who can operate where strategy, technology, and revenue intersect.
   

  

 We’re looking for a
 **Client Strategy Consultant** 
 with commercial depth, executive credibility, and sharp analytical thinking. Someone who thrives in complex environments, challenges how things have always been done, and helps leadership teams turn uncertainty into structure, clarity, and measurable revenue impact.
   

  

 What you’ll be doing
   

  

 As a Client Strategy Consultant at Upsales, you operate far beyond onboarding or system setup. You own the customer’s commercial progress over time and act as a trusted strategic advisor to CEOs, Heads of Sales, and executive teams with one clear objective: measurable revenue growth.
   

  

 You start with the business, not the tool. You analyze why sales performance is falling short by examining structure, ways of working, governance, and priorities.
   

  

 You challenge and advise leadership teams on sales strategy, process design, governance models, and commercial prioritization. You lead executive workshops, facilitate decision-making at C-level, and present clear recommendations that translate strategy into action.
   

  

 You design and implement sales processes that are directly tied to business outcomes such as revenue growth, forecast accuracy, and shorter sales cycles.
   

  

 Change leadership is a core part of the role. You drive adoption across sales managers and sales teams, track usage and performance over time, identify friction points, and address blockers that prevent insight from turning into revenue.
   

  

 You work with multiple customers in parallel, often in high-growth, high-stakes environments where expectations are high and clarity is limited.
   

  

 You bring structured customer insights back into product development and Upsales’ sales methodology, helping shape how we build, sell, and deliver the next generation of AI-powered revenue acceleration.
   

  

 This is not a delivery role. It is a role for someone who takes ownership, drives change, and wants to shape how modern B2B sales organizations grow with structure, data, and AI.
   

  

**AI first mindset**
 We’re not looking for someone who simply uses AI. We want someone who weaponizes it. You treat tools like Claude, ChatGPT, and other AI copilots as force multipliers, allowing you to deliver at a level and speed others can’t match. For you, AI isn’t an add-on, it’s the default operating system for how you think, work, and lead. You’re relentlessly curious about new capabilities, and you push the limits of what’s possible, turning AI into a competitive advantage for yourself, Upsales, and our customers. This is a role for someone with real ambition: someone who wants to set a new standard for efficiency, precision, and impact in the age of AI.
   

  

 Who we’re looking for
   

  

 You’re a senior, trusted professional. You earn credibility quickly and make complex commercial problems feel solvable.
   

  

**You Should Have**
* At least 5+ years of relevant experience from sales leadership, revenue or sales operations, management consulting with a commercial focus, or senior CRM advisory roles
* Deep understanding of B2B sales and what actually drives revenue
* The ability to diagnose complex problems and turn insight into clear direction
* Executive presence and confidence to lead CEO-level discussions and challenge assumptions
* Strong technical understanding of CRM platforms, integrations, automation, and AI-driven workflows
* Proven experience driving change and ensuring adoption, not just delivery
* A degree from a respected academic institution, preferably in the technical field
* Excellent communication skills in English and Swedish
* A high-ownership mindset. You take responsibility for outcomes, not activities


 At Upsales, you’ll work with exceptionally sharp colleagues in a true meritocracy. You’ll have autonomy, influence over our product and methodology, and a learning budget without limits. Benefits are generous, and performance is rewarded.
   

  

 If you want to be the advisor companies trust when growth really matters, and help shape the future of AI-driven B2B revenue, we’d love to meet you.
   

  

 Upsales is a bootstrapped company with big ambitions, known for our high-performing team, relentless curiosity, and a culture that challenges conventional thinking. From product to sales, we’re obsessed with creating real value for our customers and building software that actually moves the needle.

title: Senior Account Executive - FSI
description:
**Senior Account Executive - FSI - Hybrid**
 We’re a leader in data and AI. Through our software and services, we inspire customers around the world to transform data into intelligence - and questions into answers.
   

  

 If you're looking for a dynamic, fulfilling career with flexibility and a world-class employee experience, you'll find it here. We're recognized around the world for our inclusive, meaningful culture and innovative technologies by organizations like Fast Company, Forbes, Newsweek and more.
   

  

**About The Job**
 The FSI sales team is hiring a Senior Account Executive to assist customers and prospects in shaping their data and AI strategies and modernizing their solutions.
   

  

 The role involves understanding business challenges, presenting SAS solutions, and building relationships with key accounts in the Financial Services market (mainly banks). The focus will be on driving growth by leveraging existing relationships and creating new ones. This position offers a chance for an experienced sales professional to drive strategic enterprise Financial Services accounts . You’ll grow alongside a supportive team, learn from industry leaders, and make a lasting impact by turning complex challenges into meaningful solutions.
   

  

 The role will be based in Sweden (Solna) with hybrid working model and will require traveling as per business needs.
   

  

**As a Senior Account Executive – FSI, you will**
* Be responsible for achievement of total software revenue target and management business objectives.
* Engage with targeted customers and prospect accounts to position SAS solutions, managing deals typically between $100K to $1M+.
* Deliver strategies to modernize customer engagement for strategic clients.
* Build and maintain relationships, influencing long-term strategies and engaging with C-level decision-makers.
* Provide knowledge in industry, business, and marketing technology to close deals in your assigned territory.
* Support lead-generation activities and integrate strategies into account plans, qualifying based on business and technical fit.
* Develop opportunities and create compelling proposals to communicate the value of SAS solutions, collaborating with internal teams
* Support the closure of complex sales and assist with pricing and licensing negotiations.
* Embrace curiosity, passion, authenticity and accountability. These are our values and influence everything we do.


**Required Qualifications**
* Demonstrated experience in selling solutions and services to both business owners and IT stakeholders.
* Experience in identifying and solving business challenges within the targeted industries.
* Demonstrated experience in account mapping and in navigating in complex ecosystem.
* Ability to analyze and evaluate territory dynamics and develop a sales plan; ability to communicate technical and business concepts and relate them to SAS applications and user needs; ability to work independently and as part of a team.
* Knowledge of SaaS complex solution sales ideally with a focus on data analytics, AI, Generative AI. Awareness of SAS solutions and services preferred.
* Fluent in Swedish and English.
* Equivalent combination of related education, training and experience may be considered in place of the above qualifications.


**Additional Competencies, Knowledge And Skills**
* Sales Planning: Develops and executes strategic sales plans to achieve revenue targets and drive business growth.
* Customer Centricity: Prioritizes customer needs and delivers tailored solutions that enhance satisfaction and loyalty.
* Relationship Building: Cultivates strong, trust-based relationships with clients and stakeholders to foster long-term partnerships.
* Obstacle Navigation: Proactively identifies and overcomes challenges to maintain momentum and deliver results.
* Insight Driven Value Creation: Leverages data and market insights to craft compelling value propositions that address client pain points.
* Self-Motivation: Demonstrates initiative and drive to exceed goals independently in a fast-paced, target-driven environment.


**Benefits Highlights**
* You will receive a competitive salary package and fringe benefits.
* You will grow in an energizing environment – our facilities in Solna are amazing.
* You will enjoy flexibility as we operate in a hybrid model.


**You are welcome here**
 At SAS, it’s not about fitting into our culture – it’s about adding to it. We believe our people make the difference. Our inclusive workforce brings together unique talents and inspires teams to create amazing software that reflects the diversity of our users and customers.
   

  

**Additional Information**
 SAS only sends emails from verified “sas.com” email addresses and never asks for sensitive, personal information or money. If you have any doubts about the authenticity of any type of communication from, or on behalf of SAS, please contact Recruitingsupport@sas.com.
   

  

 Let's stay in touch! Join our Talent Community to stay up to date on company news, job updates and more.
   

  

 #financialservicesfeb2026

title: Growth Marketing Lead
description:
**The Mission**
 We are looking for a
 ***Founding Growth Marketing Lead***
 to architect the engine that will scale Verified across existing and new markets. Working directly with the VP Marketing, you will be a founding member of the marketing team - a "Builder-Strategist" who is world-class in driving revenue and building pipeline but also possesses the seniority to shape our broader marketing mix.
   

  

 This role requires a combo of an analytical mind and a creative mindset. You aren't just here to manage a budget; you are here to build a repeatable system, experiment with non-obvious ways to capture attention in a crowded market and act as a force multiplier for the team through AI.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
* Strategic Partnership: Work hand-in-hand with the VP Marketing to align growth experiments with the overall brand vision and company OKRs.
* Architect the Growth Engine: Own the full-funnel strategy. From initial awareness to "SQL-ready," you’ll design the journeys that convert high-ticket compliance buyers.
* The Swiss Army Growth Knife: You are channel-agnostic. Whether it’s mastering paid performance, organic loops or ABM, you deploy the right tool for the right stage of the B2B SaaS funnel.
* Creative Problem Solving: Use your creative muscles to find "non-obvious" ways to reach our ICP. Whether it’s a unique content format or a clever distribution play, you think outside the standard B2B playbook.
* AI-First Execution: You have been using AI tools for years to 10x your output. You’ll leverage AI for data analysis, content creation and workflow automation, setting the standard for efficiency across the org.
* Master the HubSpot Stack: Own the technical infrastructure. You will build the lead scoring, attribution and automation that allows us to scale efficiently without adding unnecessary headcount.
* The "Jobs-to-be-Done" Expert: Translate complex regulatory pain into high-converting copy and landing pages, prioritizing technical trust and clarity over "marketing fluff."


**Who You Are**
* The Creative Scientist: You love A/B testing and data, but you know a winning experiment starts with a creative spark. You are proactive in finding new ways to bring Verified to market.
* Scale-up Veteran: 5–7 years in B2B SaaS, preferably in a scale-up environment. You’ve seen what "good" looks like in a high-growth environment and know how to build it from (more or less) zero.
* AI-Native: You don't just "use" ChatGPT; you have integrated AI into your daily stack for years to enhance research, copy testing and operational speed.
* Specialist-Generalist: You are deeply expert in demand generation, but broad enough to contribute to product marketing, PR and brand strategy.


**Why Join Verified?**
 The Journey: We are on a high-growth trajectory, backed by the investment firm Verdane, targeting a massive market with high ambitions.
   

  

 The Impact: You are the founding growth hire. You will have the freedom and responsibility to build a high-performing engine and drive real change.
   

  

 Culture of Excellence: We value builders. If you thrive on autonomy, technical mastery, and the challenge of scaling a technical product, you’ll find your home here.

title: Solution Architect Digital Workplace
description:
Beskrivning av tjänsten
   

  

 Vill du vara med och forma framtidens digitala arbetsplatser för några av Sveriges mest betydelsefulla organisationer?
   

  

 CGI söker nu en erfaren Solution Architect inom Digital Workplace som vill arbeta nära våra kunder i bid‑ och upphandlingsteam. Rollen passar dig som trivs i gränslandet mellan teknik, affär och kunddialog och som vill ta en central och strategisk funktion i våra erbjudanden.
   

  

 Vi välkomnar sökande från flera orter, bland annat Mälardalen, Göteborgsområdet och Skåne.
   

  

**Om rollen**
 Som Solution Architect hos CGI får du en viktig och strategisk roll i våra affärsprojekt. Du ansvarar för att utforma lösningar för moderna digitala arbetsplatser och säkerställa att de uppfyller kundernas krav på säkerhet, användarupplevelse, produktivitet och kostnadseffektivitet.
   

  

 Arbetet sker nära våra kundteam, projektledare och tekniska specialister, där du bidrar med både tekniskt och affärsmässigt perspektiv från första analys till färdigt lösningsförslag.
   

  

 Dina framtida arbetsuppgifter
   

  

 Utforma och dokumentera lösningar för moderna digitala arbetsplatser
   

  

 Ta fram lösningsbeskrivningar, kalkyler och offertunderlag
   

  

 Medverka i upphandlingar (RFP/RFI/RFQ) och anbudsarbete
   

  

 Säkerställa helheten i lösningsförslag – antaganden, risker och tekniska beroenden
   

  

 Presentera och förklara lösningar i dialog med kund
   

  

 Bidra till utveckling av CGI:s erbjudanden, metoder och standardisering
   

  

 Kvalifikationer för att bli framgångsrik denna roll
   

  

**Tekniska fokusområden (erfarenhet inom några av dessa är tillräckligt)**
 Microsoft 365 (Teams, SharePoint, Exchange, Intune, Entra ID)
   

  

 Säker digital arbetsplats, t.ex. identity & access, enhetsskydd och informationsskydd
   

  

 Endpoint management och modern klienthantering
   

  

 Digital Employee Experience (DEX)
   

  

 AI‑stöd i arbetsplatslösningar, t.ex. Copilot
   

  

 Molnbaserade och hybrida IT‑miljöer
   

  

**Kvalifikationer**
 Minst 5 års erfarenhet i roller som Solution Architect, teknisk presales, teknisk KAM eller liknande
   

  

 Erfarenhet av upphandlingar och offertarbete
   

  

 God teknisk kompetens inom Microsofts ekosystem för modern arbetsplats
   

  

 Erfarenhet av att ta fram strukturerade lösnings- och kalkylunderlag
   

  

 Mycket goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska
   

  

 Vana att hantera flera parallella affärsspå
   

  

**Meriterande:**
 Erfarenhet av större outsourcing‑ eller managed service‑affärer
   

  

 Kompetens inom säkerhetsarkitektur för digital arbetsplats
   

  

 Akademisk examen inom IT, teknik eller ekonomi
   

  

**Vi tror att du är en person som:**
 Kommunicerar tydligt och pedagogiskt – både muntligt och skriftligt
   

  

 Kan växla mellan kundperspektiv, affärsnytta och tekniska detaljer
   

  

 Arbetar strukturerat och tar ansvar för helheten i dina leveranser
   

  

 Bygger förtroende i dialoger med kunder och kollegor
   

  

**Varför CGI?**
 En affärsnära roll där du får stor påverkan på kundaffärer
   

  

 Möjlighet att arbeta med komplexa och samhällsviktiga kunder
   

  

 Samarbetsfokuserade kollegor med hög teknisk kompetens
   

  

 Kontinuerlig kompetensutveckling och tydliga karriärvägar
   

  

 Delägarskap – du blir CGI Partner, inte bara anställd
   

  

 Aktiv satsning på AI, DEX och framtidens arbetsplatslösningar
   

  

 Förmåner i urval
   

  

 Föräldralön, aktieprogram, 4 000 kr i friskvårdsbidrag, kollektivavtal, attraktiva pensionslösningar och möjlighet till personalbil.
   

  

 Ansökan
   

  

 Låter detta som nästa steg i din karriär? Skicka in din ansökan – vi arbetar med löpande urval.
   

  

 Inför anställning genomför vi UC‑kontroll och begär utdrag ur belastningsregistret.
   

  

 Frågor om rollen? Daniel Kommes, Director Consulting Delivery, daniel.kommes@cgi.com
   

  

**Låt oss som ägare omvandla verklig insikt till handling – tillsammans.**
 Livet på CGI bygger på ägande, lagarbete, respekt och tillhörighet. Tillsammans med oss kommer du att kunna nå din fulla potential.
   

  

 Du är inbjuden att ta ägandeskap redan från första dagen i vårt arbete med att förverkliga vår dröm om att skapa en arbetsmiljö där vi trivs med att arbeta tillsammans och bidrar till framgången för vårt företag. Det är därför vi kallar oss CGI Partners snarare än anställda. Vi drar nytta av vår kollektiva framgång och tillsammans får vi möjlighet att aktivt forma vår företagsstrategi och inriktning.
   

  

 Ditt arbete skapar värde. Du kommer att vara med att utveckla innovativa lösningar och bygga relationer med medarbetare och kunder samtidigt som du får tillgång till globala möjligheter att skala upp dina idéer, omfamna nya möjligheter och dra nytta av gedigen bransch- och teknikexpertis.
   

  

 Du kommer att forma din karriär tillsammans med ett företag som är byggt för att växa och bestå. Du får stöd av ledare som bryr sig om din hälsa och ditt välbefinnande och ger dig möjligheter att fördjupa dina kunskaper och vidga dina vyer.
   

  

 Bli en del en av världens största leverantörer av IT- och affärskonsulttjänster.

title: GRC Specialist (Governance, Risk & Compliance)
description:
**Om rollen**

 Säkerhet är Getswishs högsta prioritet, och vi söker nu en driven och nyfiken GRC-specialist för att stärka vår team. Som GRC-specialist på Getswish blir du en central kraft i arbetet med att strukturera, förvalta och framtidssäkra vår informationssäkerhet i ett snabbrörligt regulatoriskt landskap. Du kommer ingå i Säkerhetsfunktionen, ledd av Chief Information Security Officer, tillsammans med Information Security Officer, Säkerhetsarkitekter och Beredskaps- & Kontinuitetsansvarig,
 





 Du kommer arbeta brett inom säkerhetsrelaterade områden och med ett tätt samarbete med Legal, DPO och Risk and Compliance, spela en nyckelroll i att bygga en stark, trygg och framåtlutad säkerhetskultur inom Getswishs organisation.
   







**Arbetsuppgifter**


*I rollen ingår arbete som:* 




* Deltaga i det dagliga arbetet med informationssäkerhet med CISO och Säkerhetsfunktionen
* Säkerställa att policys, riktlinjer och instruktioner är uppdaterade, relevanta och efterlevs
* Stötta verksamheten i att modellera och dokumentera processer & kontroller
* Koordinera och facilitera interna och externa revisioner samt stötta i att ta fram åtgärdsplaner och följa upp dessa
* Driva och utveckla arbetet med regulatorisk efterlevnad, exempelvis kopplat till Finansinspektionens regelverk
* Ta fram rapportering och beslutsunderlag till ledning och styrelse inom risk och compliance




**Vi söker dig som har:** 




* Universitetsexamen eller motsvarande inom IT, informationssäkerhet eller motsvarande alternativt tillgodosedd relevant erfarenhet genom arbetslivserfarenhet
* Minst 2 års erfarenhet att arbeta med Governance, Risk & Compliance, informationssäkerhet eller IT-risk
* Erfarenhet av att arbeta med ett ledningssystem för informationssäkerhet och internationella standarder och ramverk såsom ISO/IEC27001 eller NIST
* God förståelse för regulatoriska krav såsom GDPR, DORA och Finansinspektionens föreskrifter
* Erfarenhet av revisioner, kontrolluppföljning och regulatorisk rapportering




**Vi tror att du:** 




* Har mycket god förmåga att uttrycka dig i tal och skrift, på svenska liksom på engelska.
* Är ansvarstagande och kommunicerar med tydlighet och skapar på så sätt förståelse, framdrift och förtroende för frågor inom informationssäkerhet och dataskydd.
* Har erfarenhet att leda och driva informationssäkerhetsarbete, gärna från finansiell sektor
* Kan sätta dig in i nya regelverk och leda arbetet internt för att säkerställa efterlevnad.
* Trivs och frodas i en modern och snabbfotad miljö.



**Vad vi kan erbjuda dig:** 



* Kul på jobbet med grymma kollegor som stöttar och utmanar varandra.
* En dynamisk arbetsmiljö med stor frihet att utforska nya idéer, lösningar och arbetssätt.
* En fin gemenskap över hela företaget med fokus på kultur och sociala aktiviteter - allt ifrån AW’s med föreläsningar, träning och goda fredagsfrukostar.
* Trevliga kontor som ligger centralt i både Stockholm och Östersund samt möjligheten till hybrid-arbete.
* Hälsoförsäkringar, friskvård och mycket mer för ditt välmående.
* Kollektivavtal och tjänstepension.



**Rekryteringen sker löpande, skicka in din ansökan redan idag!**
*I vår beredskapsplanering ingår att alla tillsvidareanställda är krigsplacerade i bolaget. I praktiken innebär det att du vid höjd beredskap i samhället arbetar vidare i din ordinarie roll, med särskild prioritet att stötta verksamhetens samhällsviktiga funktion.* 




**Om Swish**
 Swish lanserades 2012 och har sedan dess blivit hela Sveriges betalapp och en av de största mobila betalningslösningarna i Europa. Bolaget Getswish AB ansvarar för Swish infrastruktur, tjänster samt varumärket Swish, som på kort tid blivit Sveriges starkaste och mest omtyckta.
 



 Getswish jobbar dagligen med att förenkla vardagen för 8,7 miljoner privatpersoner och 300 000 företag. Getswish har cirka 115 medarbetare och kontor i centrala Stockholm och Östersund.
 



 Getswish utvecklar kontinuerligt nya produkter, förbättrar existerande tjänster och utvärderar innovativa idéer. Vi är ett litet passionerat bolag med höga ambitioner att tillhandahålla tjänster och funktioner i framkant.

title: Solution Architect (Lead-to-Revenue)
description:
**Solution Architect (Lead-to-Revenue)** 




**ABOUT SINCH**
 Sinch is pioneering the way the world communicates. More than 150,000 businesses - including Google, Uber, Paypal, Visa, Tinder, and many others - rely on Sinch's Customer Communications Cloud to power engaging customer experiences through mobile messaging, voice, and email.
   

 Whether you need to verify users or craft omnichannel campaigns, Sinch makes it easy. Our AI-infused Super Network, APIs, and applications ensure you can connect with your customers reliably and securely, at every step of their journey.
   

 At Sinch we "Dream Big", "Win Together", "Keep it simple", and "Make it Happen". These values are our foundation!
 



**DESCRIPTION**
 We are seeking an experienced architect to take on this high-impact role as Solution Architect to design and govern end-to-end Lead-to-Revenue (L2R) processes and Sales & Marketing domain architecture - essentially to shape the very processes that power our growth! The key mission is to align business strategy with scalable processes, integration patterns, and enterprise standards across the revenue ecosystem.
 



**Key Responsibilities** 



* Architect and optimize L2R and customer lifecycle processes.
* Define domain architecture, integration patterns, and data ownership models.
* Establish canonical data models and scalable API/event-driven integrations.
* Provide architectural governance across CRM and revenue platforms.
* Partner with business and IT to drive transformation initiatives.



**Qualifications** 



* Enterprise/domain architecture experience in Sales, Marketing, or Revenue.
* Strong L2R and CRM ecosystem expertise (Salesforce preferred).
* Integration architecture knowledge (APIs, middleware, event-driven).
* Strong stakeholder management and systems thinking.



**ABOUT OUR HIRING PROCESS**
 Sinch is a global company composed of people from different countries and cultures. Our benefits adjust regionally to support employees and help them to thrive in every stage of life. We offer valuable benefits and resources, including health and life insurance, a flexible work environment, retirement savings plans, and more.
   

 In Sinch, we are committed to following a recruitment process that is fair, objective, consistent, and equal opportunity. Our Talent Acquisition team, together with hiring managers and the rest of the interviewing team, persistently works towards identifying the people that best fit each open job, based on Sinch' s hiring needs and candidates' career expectations.
   

 We encourage applications from strong candidates with relevant professional backgrounds for this role. Not all applicants will meet all job requirements exactly! Don't let that stop you from considering Sinch for the next step in your career. We are always open to candidates that could bring new insights and perspectives to Sinch!
 



**Please send your CV in English language.**

title: Scrum Master - Stockholm
description:
Beskrivning av tjänsten
   

  

 Har du ett starkt engagemang för att leda team, driva kontinuerliga förbättringar och skapa meningsfullt värde i agila utvecklingsprojekt? Då kan du vara den Scrum Master vi söker!
   

  

 Vi befinner oss i en spännande fas och söker nu fler engagerade Scrum Masters som vill vara med och utveckla både team och arbetssätt. I rollen kommer du att coacha och stötta tvärfunktionella team i våra kunders utvecklingsprojekt. Du skapar goda förutsättningar för teamets leverans genom att undanröja hinder, facilitera samarbete och säkerställa ett effektivt arbetssätt.
   

  

 Som Scrum Master har du en central roll i teamet och samarbetar tätt med Produktägare och utvecklare.
   

  

 Rollens ansvar och fokusområden inkluderar:
   

  

* Facilitera dagliga stand-ups, sprintplanering, demos och retrospektiv
* Stödja produktägaren i backloghantering och kravställning
* Driva förbättringar i teamets processer och arbetsmetodik
* Bidra till ett hållbart tempo och en kultur av ständig förbättring


 Du kommer att verka i en miljö där samarbete, professionalism och delaktighet står i fokus. Inom vår enhet Manufacturing & Transportation har vi lång erfarenhet av förvaltning och vidareutveckling av affärskritiska applikationer, ofta i nära samarbete med några av Sveriges största företag – inom ett brett spektrum av branscher.
   

  

 Hos oss får du möjlighet att växa i din roll, utbyta erfarenheter med kunniga kollegor och vara med och forma framtidens IT-landskap tillsammans med våra kunder. Vår kultur präglas av nyfikenhet, engagemang och en stark vilja att alltid bli lite bättre – varje dag.
   

  

 Dina framtida arbetsuppgifter
   

  

* Utföra typiska Scrum Master-uppgifter, såsom att facilitera Scrum-ceremonier, leda backlog refinement och driva kontinuerliga förbättringar utifrån retroperspektiv.
* Identifiera och undanröja hinder som påverkar teamets framdrift.
* Etablera, utveckla och underhålla starka och effektiva relationer med olika intressenter.
* Underlätta teamdiskussioner och bidra till att hantera och lösa eventuella konflikter.
* Aktivt delta i affärsutveckling genom att identifiera nya affärsmöjligheter och föreslå förbättringar i både produkt och arbetsmetodik.
* Coacha och stötta teamledare och teammedlemmar i deras professionella utveckling och bidra till att bygga högpresterande team.


 Kvalifikationer för att bli framgångsrik denna roll
   

  

 För att bli framgångsrik i denna roll ser vi att du har en god analytisk förmåga, är skicklig på att prioritera och har ett helhetsperspektiv i ditt ledarskap. Du är en närvarande och lyhörd ledare som skapar engagemang och trygghet i teamet, samtidigt som du driver förbättringar och leveranser framåt.
   

  

 Vi söker dig som har:
   

  

* Minst två (4) års erfarenhet av att arbeta som Scrum Master
* Certifierad som Scrum Master samt meriterande med certifiering inom SCRUM.org, SAFe, PMI-ACP eller andra agila ramverk
* Praktisk erfarenhet av att arbeta enligt SAFe eller andra agila metoder
* Meriterande med erfarenhet av kravhantering och/eller kvalitetsarbete (QA)


 Du har ett starkt engagemang för agila metoder och brinner för att skapa högpresterande team. Som person är du en lagspelare som motiveras av att coacha andra och driva teamets utveckling. Du är bra på att hantera målkonflikter, kommunicerar tydligt och skapar förtroende i dina samarbeten. Du växlar mellan helhetsperspektiv och detaljfokus och är både lösningsorienterad och leveranssäker i ditt arbetssätt.
   

  

 Låter det här som en roll för dig? Välkommen att bli en del av ett av CGI's snabbast växande team – skicka in din ansökan redan idag!
   

  

**Vill du veta mer?**
 Vi ser fram emot att höra från dig! Vi hanterar ansökningar löpande så skicka in din ansökan redan i dag! Om du har frågor om rollen, hör gärna av dig till rekryterande ledare Giliam Oostema, Director Consulting Services/ Konsultchef, på giliam.oostema@cgi.com
   

  

 Personliga säkerhetskrav kommer att krävas om rollen kräver det.
   

  

**Vad CGI erbjuder dig**
 CGIs kombination av global och lokal närvaro, med kontor på 30 orter i Sverige, ger nöjda kunder men innebär även att du som anställd alltid har ett kontor nära dig. Detta i kombination med flexibla arbetstider ger dig möjlighet till balans mellan privatliv och arbete, oavsett var i livet just du befinner dig.
   

  

 På CGI finns även stora möjligheter till interna karriärvägar där du kan välja att bli specialist inom ditt område, bredda dig inom nya områden eller ta en ledande befattning. Via våra interna nätverk, projekt och åtaganden i globala och nationella industrier, erbjuder vi dig kompetensutveckling och engagerade ledare som stöttar just dig i din utveckling.
   

  

 Mer information om våra förmåner:
   

  

* Aktieprogram och delägarskap: Som delägare har du möjlighet att påverka och i vårt aktieprogram matchar CGI ditt bidrag, krona för krona upp till 3% av din månadslön.
* Friskvårdsbidrag: Vi erbjuder ett årligt friskvårdsbidrag på 4000 kr.
* Föräldralön: Vi betalar upp till cirka 90% av din lön under den första tiden hemma.
* Trygg anställning: Våra konsulter anställs med kollektivavtal och försäkringar.
* Pensionslösningar: Vi erbjuder attraktiva och förmånliga pensionslösningar.
* Personalbil: Personalbilen används som en privatbil men leasas av CGI.


**Låt oss som ägare omvandla verklig insikt till handling – tillsammans.**
 Livet på CGI bygger på ägande, lagarbete, respekt och tillhörighet. Tillsammans med oss kommer du att kunna nå din fulla potential.
   

  

 Du är inbjuden att ta ägandeskap redan från första dagen i vårt arbete med att förverkliga vår dröm om att skapa en arbetsmiljö där vi trivs med att arbeta tillsammans och bidrar till framgången för vårt företag. Det är därför vi kallar oss CGI Partners snarare än anställda. Vi drar nytta av vår kollektiva framgång och tillsammans får vi möjlighet att aktivt forma vår företagsstrategi och inriktning.
   

  

 Ditt arbete skapar värde. Du kommer att vara med att utveckla innovativa lösningar och bygga relationer med medarbetare och kunder samtidigt som du får tillgång till globala möjligheter att skala upp dina idéer, omfamna nya möjligheter och dra nytta av gedigen bransch- och teknikexpertis.
   

  

 Du kommer att forma din karriär tillsammans med ett företag som är byggt för att växa och bestå. Du får stöd av ledare som bryr sig om din hälsa och ditt välbefinnande och ger dig möjligheter att fördjupa dina kunskaper och vidga dina vyer.
   

  

 Bli en del en av världens största leverantörer av IT- och affärskonsulttjänster.

title: Associate - Capital Markets (Junior Level)
description:
White & Case's award-winning Capital Markets Practice has a market-leading presence in all major financial centers across the Americas, EMEA and APAC. Our heritage, scale, comprehensive offering, deep US/English law bench and fully integrated global network make us a first choice for complex cross-border capital markets matters. We cover the full spectrum of securities products: debt and equity capital markets, high yield, structured finance and derivatives, securitization, and corporate trust. Our client base includes all leading global investment banks, NYSE, NASDAQ and FTSE-listed corporates, sovereigns and funds (both private equity and distressed). What sets us apart is our history of delivering groundbreaking capital markets advice in mature and growth markets, which translates to fresh thinking and original solutions. Over many years, we have built up considerable institutional knowledge, advising on thousands of securities transactions around the globe.
   

  

****Vill du vara med och bygga världens ledande affärsjuridiska advokatbyrå?****
**Är du i slutet av din juristutbildning eller har du nyligen tagit juristexamen? White & Case söker associates till Capital Markets** 
**med start i slutet av augusti 2026.**
**Om oss**
 White & Case är en av världens största och främsta advokatbyråer. Sedan starten i New York 1901 har vi expanderat internationellt och etablerat 43 kontor i 29 länder. Många av våra klienter är multinationella organisationer med komplexa behov som kräver engagemang från flera av våra kontor samtidigt. Det betyder att vi arbetar i en dynamisk miljö med kollegor från hela världen och har gedigen erfarenhet av internationell affärsjuridik. Våra specialistområden på Stockholmskontoret inkluderar M&A/Corporate, M&A/Private Equity, Capital Markets, Debt Finance och Disputes.
   

  

**Vem vi söker**
 Vi söker dig som snart ska ta, eller nyligen har tagit, din juristexamen och har ett genuint intresse för affärsjuridik. För att trivas hos oss tycker du om att arbeta projektbaserat, kommersiellt och i team. Vi tror även att du är en lagspelare och tycker om att utvecklas snabbt. Examen i företagsekonomi, tingstjänstgöring eller tidigare relevant arbetslivserfarenhet kan vara meriterande men är inget krav. Eftersom vi är en internationell byrå behöver du, utöver svenska, även ha goda kunskaper i engelska.
   

  

 Vi letar alltid efter individer som kan bidra med olika perspektiv och erfarenheter för att gemensamt leverera förstklassig service till våra klienter. Vi lovar att du kommer utvecklas snabbt i din roll som associate samtidigt som du kommer ha roligt och skapa både relationer och kontakter för livet.
   

  

**Rollen som associate**
 Som associate i vår Capital Markets grupp kommer du att vara en viktig del av ett sammansvetsat team med bred erfarenhet inom sitt verksamhetsområde. Vi arbetar med en mängd olika och ofta högprofilerade kapitalmarknadstransaktioner, exempelvis börsintroduktioner, kapitalanskaffningar i börsnoterade bolag, publik M&A samt löpande corporate governance-frågor.
   

  

**Varför White & Case**
 På vårt Stockholmskontor är vi 130 medarbetare och arbetar efter våra värderingar Pioneering, United och Human. Vi strävar alltid efter att utvecklas och gör det helst tillsammans. Vi är en integrerad del av en av världens största och främsta advokatfirmor och har därmed unika förutsättningar att hantera de största och mest komplexa transaktionerna, processerna och andra affärsjuridiska frågor. På White & Case arbetar du i en organisation med täta kontakter med våra andra kontor där det är lika naturligt att prata med, och träffa, kollegor i London, New York, Paris eller Frankfurt, som kollegorna i Stockholm och Helsingfors.
   

  

**Rekryteringsprocessen**
 Vill du bli en del av vårt team? Ansök med ditt CV (på engelska), personligt brev (på engelska) samt betyg från juristprogrammet. Vi rekryterar löpande till tjänsten. Har du frågor om tjänsten är du välkommen att kontakta Head of HR, Petra Lindeblad petra.lindeblad@whitecase.com, Talent Acquisition & HR Advisor, Cajsa Billebjer cajsa.billebjer@whitecase.com eller partner Johan Thiman johan.thiman@whitecase.com
   

  

**Placering och rapportering**
 Placeringsort: Biblioteksgatan 12, Stockholm
   

  

 Rapporterar till: Ansvarig partner på Capital Markets, Stockholm
   

  

**This role reports to**
 Partner
   

  

**Equal Opportunities**
 White & Case is committed to creating a fair workplace. It is our Firm’s policy to recruit, employ, train, compensate, and promote without regard to race, religion, creed, national origin, age, gender, sexual orientation, marital status, military or veteran status, disability, genetic information, or any other category protected by applicable law.
   

  

 If you require assistance and/or adjustment to participate in our application and/or interview process, please email us. We will be happy to work with you.
   

  

 The Firm may modify and amend any job description at any time in its sole discretion. Nothing herein creates a contract of employment or otherwise modifies the at-will nature of employment.
   

  

 The above is only a general description of the essential duties associated with this position and does not represent an exhaustive or comprehensive list of all duties.
   

  

**Note to Recruitment Agencies**
 Our internal Recruitment team manages all aspects of lateral hiring. All agencies must have signed terms of business—specific to the relevant office—before submitting any candidates. CVs or applications sent directly to White & Case partners or employees will also not be considered formal introductions. If you have questions, please contact the relevant Recruitment team. We work with our preferred suppliers when engaging agencies.

title: Assortment Designer - H&M Mens - Knit & Jersey
description:
**Job Description**

**Assortment Designer – Menswear**




 Are you an accomplished Design Leader with a sharp eye for style and a proven ability to achieve standout results? We are looking for an experienced Designer to lead our Menswear Knitwear & Jersey department.
   

  





 If you thrive on shaping industry-leading products and are motivated to drive growth and excellence, this is the role for you.
   

  





 As an Assortment Designer for Menswear, you play a pivotal role—turning trend insights, customer needs, and brand identity into a lineup that is creatively compelling and commercially strong.
   

  





 In this role, you’ll own the product strategy from concept through final collection, working closely with design, buying, merchandising, and production teams to deliver a cohesive, profitable, and brand-aligned assortment. Your ability to combine creative vision with data-driven decision-making will be essential in shaping products that connect with customers across global markets.
   

  





**This position is based at our HQ in Stockholm, Sweden.**




**What You’ll Do...**



* Drive the overall creative direction of our Menswear Knit & Jersey, ensuring it aligns with the brand’s vision and delivers on customer expectations.
* Develop bold, forward-thinking concepts that set a strong creative tone and fuel new ideas across the design team.
* Work closely with Designers, Product Developers & Planners to build a balanced, innovative, and commercially powerful product mix.
* Leverage customer insights, market research, trend intelligence, and sales performance to shape winning product strategies and compelling seasonal stories.
* Maintain a cohesive, relevant, and competitive assortment across all markets.
* Lead a team of approx 15 people across design and print/graphics with creativity, collaboration, and a commitment to continuous improvement across the team.
* Present the vision and assortment strategy with confidence, clarity, and a commanding storytelling presence.



**Who are we looking for...**



* A senior menswear designer/leader with a degree in fashion design (BA/MA) or equivalent experience, backed by 10+ years in the industry.
* Experience of staff responsibility is highly preferred.
* A creative, strategic thinker with sharp fashion instincts and a solid understanding of global trends.
* Experienced in assortment planning, concept development, and collection building—ideally within a fast-paced, high-demand environment.
* Able to merge strong creative intuition with solid commercial judgment.
* A confident communicator who can motivate teams and deliver a clear, compelling vision.
* Highly collaborative, proactive, and decisive—comfortable owning decisions and moving projects forward with purpose.



 Through your leadership and expertise, you will secure the execution of assortment strategies from idea to finished product, continuously optimizing for customer relevance and profitability.
   

  





**Who we are....**




 H&M Group is a global company of strong fashion brands and ventures. Our goal is to prove that there is no compromise between exceptional design, affordable prices, and sustainable solutions. We want to liberate fashion for the many, and our customers are at the heart of every decision we make.​
   

  





 We are made up of thousands of passionate and talented colleagues united by our shared culture and values. Together, we want to use our power, our scale, and our knowledge to push the fashion industry towards a more inclusive and sustainable future. ​
   

  





 Our uniqueness comes from a combination of many things – our inclusive and collaborative culture, our strong values, and opportunities for growth. But most of all, it’s our people who make us who we are.​
   

  





**Please apply with your latest Portfolio and CV as soon as possible to be considered for this opportunity.**




 Take the next step in your career together with us. The journey starts here.​

title: Information Architect, Master Data Management - Machining
description:
At Business Area Machining within Sandvik, we're now strengthening the information structures that support our master data and integration landscape. In this role, you take the lead in shaping how shared data is modeled, connected, and understood across the organization — work that enables a scalable and future‑ready digital ecosystem.
   

  

**What’s in it for you?**
* The opportunity to influence Business Area Machining’s information architecture and data foundations
* A role that works across brands, functions, and technologies
* A meaningful part in our digital transformation journey


**About The Job**
 As Information Architect – Master Data Management, you design sustainable, scalable information models that support Machining’s master data management (MDM). You ensure that shared information structures align with business needs, enterprise architecture, and data governance frameworks.
   

  

 Your role includes establishing canonical models and metadata standards, supporting semantic alignment across divisions, and ensuring that data flows across the PLM–MDM–ERP landscape are meaningful and interoperable. You collaborate closely with IT, business stakeholders, and data stewards to build shared understanding and ownership of information assets, while guiding the transition toward target-state architecture.
   

  

 Key parts of the job:
   

  

* Designing and implementing canonical models, metadata structures, and information blueprints
* Supporting shared information models and semantic alignment across Machining
* Ensuring interoperable, traceable data flows across core systems
* Facilitating collaboration between IT, business, and data stewards
* Contributing to transition and coexistence models as legacy systems evolve
* Connecting integration models with master data definitions to enable a composable digital backbone


**Location and flexibility**
 The position is based in Stockholm or Sandviken, Sweden and includes some international travel. You report to the Head of CIO Office at Machining.
   

  

**Profile**
 You have a deep understanding of information modeling and data integration design, along with an analytical and structured mindset. You translate business and technical needs into clear, reusable models and enjoy making complex concepts understandable.
   

  

 Success in this role builds on being analytical, curious, and pragmatic — someone who collaborates well across functions and helps bring clarity to complex information landscapes.
   

  

 You bring:
   

  

* Strong knowledge of information modeling, semantic modeling, and data integration
* Familiarity with PLM, ERP, and MDM ecosystems, and understanding of data flows and governance
* Experience with UML and established modeling notations
* Skills in workshop facilitation and an agile approach to information design
* A degree in Information Systems, Computer Science, Information Architecture, or similar
* Curiosity about modern data‑architecture concepts such as composability, data fabric, or semantic interoperability
* Strong communication skills in English (Swedish is a plus)


**Some Words About Your New Team**
 Business area Machining brings together expertise from multiple brands, and collaboration is part of our everyday rhythm. The team you join is committed to building the foundations that make product and material information reliable, accessible, and ready for the future. It’s work that enables smarter manufacturing, stronger decision-making, and a scaled digital journey across Sandvik.
   

  

**Our culture**
 At Sandvik, we’re tech driven, innovative, and entrepreneurial. We believe success is a team effort, and we’re committed to building an inclusive environment where everyone can be themselves and reach their full potential. We support each other, learn together, and value the different perspectives that make us stronger. Visit our stories hub, LinkedIn or Facebook to get to know us further.
   

  

**Contact information**
 For further information about this position, please contact Anna-Karin Larsson, hiring manager, at anna-karin.larsson@sandvik.com.
   

  

 We’ve already decided on which advertising channels and marketing campaigns we wish to use and respectfully decline any additional contacts in that matter.
   

  

 Union contacts – Sweden
   

  

* Thomas Lilja, Unionen, +46 (0)70 261 04 82
* Tord Engström, Akademikerföreningen, +46 (0)76 106 62 87
* Peter Olsson-Andrée, Ledarna, +46 (0)70 222 48 55


 Recruitment Specialist: Hanna Thomas
   

  

**How To Apply**
 Send your application no later than March 6, 2026. Click apply and include your resume and cover letter in English. Please note that we don’t accept applications by e-mail. Job ID: R0088302.
   

  

 As we aim for a fair recruitment process, we utilize assessment tools to safeguard objectivity. When you apply for this job, you will therefore receive an invitation via email to a personality and logic ability test. Feedback comes immediately after the test has been completed and the selection process begins after the application deadline.
   

  

 To learn more about our recruitment process, please visit our career site or contact HR Services at hrservices.sweden@sandvik.com.
   

  

*Sandvik is a global, high-tech engineering group providing solutions that enhance productivity, profitability and sustainability for the manufacturing, mining and infrastructure industries. We are at the forefront of digitalization and focus on optimizing our customers’ processes. Our world-leading offering includes equipment, tools, services and digital solutions for machining, mining, rock excavation and rock processing. In 2024, the group had approximately 41,000 employees, sales in more than 150 countries and revenues of about SEK 123 billion.*

title: Product Partnership Engineer
description:
**TL;DR:** 
 We’re hiring a Product Partnership Engineer to build and scale Lovable’s most strategic product partnerships. You’ll work directly with enterprise partners to design and ship high-impact integrations, reading their stack, prototyping solutions yourself, and shaping both our roadmap and theirs. This role sits at the intersection of engineering, product, and enterprise relationships, and will directly accelerate Lovable’s ecosystem growth and enterprise adoption.
   

  

**Why Lovable?**
 Lovable lets anyone and everyone build software with any language. From solopreneurs to Fortune 100 teams, millions of people use Lovable to transform raw ideas into real products - fast. We are at the forefront of a foundational shift in software creation, which means you have an unprecedented opportunity to change the way the digital world works. Over 2 million people in 200+ countries already use Lovable to launch businesses, automate work, and bring their ideas to life. And we’re just getting started.
   

  

 We’re a small, talent-dense team building a generation-defining company from Stockholm. We value extreme ownership, high velocity, and low-ego collaboration. We seek out people who care deeply, ship fast, and are eager to make a dent in the world.
   

  

**What We’re Looking For**
* 5+ years in Technical Business Development, Forward Deployed Engineering, Solutions Engineering, or as a technical founder/CTO - in high-velocity environments.
* Hands-on experience with integrations; you've read the docs, written the scripts, and ship something to production, even if you're not a full-time engineer.
* Track record of structuring and executing product-centric partnerships that involve real technical depth.
* Genuinely technical; comfortable reading technical documentation, understanding cloud architectures, and forming independent views on integration feasibility.
* Strong enterprise and commercial instincts: comfortable navigating senior stakeholders in negotiating, building relationships and managing complex deals while maintaining technical credibility.
* High agency and bias for action; you prototype first and refine later than waiting for full specs.


**What You’ll Do**
* Own and deepen relationships with Lovable’s most strategic product and enterprise partners — driving urgency, alignment, and executive-level momentum.
* Independently assess partner architectures, APIs, and other integration points to identify high-impact integration opportunities, validate feasibility, and proactively surface risks or blockers.
* Prototype and spike early integrations yourself to shape requirements, accelerate decision-making, and reduce engineering lift.
* Scope and, where appropriate, ship production-ready integrations that deliver measurable value to Lovable users and our partners.
* Develop compelling business cases for integrations that clearly demonstrate long-term value creation for partners, Lovable, and end customers with a strong focus on measurable impact.
* Feed technical and market insights back into Lovable’s product organization, while collaborating cross-functionally (Engineering, Product, Marketing, Growth) and leading workshops to drive alignment and successful go-to-market execution.


**About Your Application**
 Please submit your application in English. It’s our company language, so you’ll be speaking lots of it if you join.
   

  

 We treat all candidates equally - if you’re interested, please apply through our careers portal.

title: Sales Engineer Chillers -Hydronics-
description:
Are you looking for a new opportunity that offers meaningful work with true purpose? We would love to welcome you to our multinational team!
   

 Your growth is our growth. We invest in your future by enhancing your skills and abilities through training opportunities and interesting challenges.
   

  

 At Panasonic, we believe in the power of collaboration - only together can we continue to expand our innovative edge and exceed our customers’ expectations. We are a powerful brand with a rich company culture, based on our 7 principles. It's not just about what we do, but how we do it. To fulfil our social responsibilities, we will continue to do what we have done for over 100 years - to enrich people’s lives at home and at work, and move society forward.
   

  





  

**WHAT YOU WILL BE DOING**
* Identify and visit target consultants, architects, main contactors and installers in order to identify projects. Support the consultants and the installers on designing the projects with Panasonic solutions.
* Ensure customer satisfaction by coordinating with internal and external partners.
* Follow-up of the projects on Salesforce, and follow-up of the project pipeline on a regular basis.
* Support the installers and consultants with trainings regarding the products and software for the responsible product category.
* Feedback the market information to the Product Managers, and to reflect into own strategy/tactics review and the future product development. Suggest appropriate measures to achieve the strategic company goals.
* Identify and built strong relationships with specifiers, design offices, and installers in order to increase the project business.
* Coordinate regularly with relevant internal partners to follow up projects installations from product sales to its commissioning, in order to minimize risk of installation problems by predicting and anticipating any delicate project issue and taking appropriate preventive measures to better ensure the success of customer service.
* Ensure appropriate documentation of the project pipeline and project progress in the relevant systems.






  

**WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR**
* Relevant University Degree or equivalent practical and vocational knowledge and experience
* 3-5 years of relevant experience in the HVAC sales
* Experience in Business Development in hydronics






  

**WHAT WE OFFER YOU**
* A challenging strategic role where you can actively contribute to the company's success and take on responsibility
* International work environment
* Variety of training and development opportunities
* Attractive remuneration package, social, fitness & health benefits
* Flexible working hours
* Generous mobile working option
* High Work-Life Balance






  

**ABOUT US /O NAS / ÜBER UNS**
 Pana­sonic is one of the largest and lead­ing elec­tronic pro­duct manu­facturers in the world. The depth and variety of our re­search capa­bilities, manu­facturing ex­pertise, high quality and sophisticated pro­ducts have remained strong since 1918. Our mission is to create the Panasonic business of "tomorrow." By integrating business, technology, and creativity, we will realize ideal lifestyles and societies solving social issues globally. Learn more about Sustainability at Panasonic.
   

  

 Please find here more information about Panasonic as an employer.
   





  

**DIVERSITY, EQUITY AND INCLUSION**
 Fairness and equality are in our DNA. We are committed to creating a safe and inclusive workplace where people feel empowered to bring their whole selves to work. We want to ensure that our people are respected for who they are as individuals, valued for what they do and celebrated for their contribution to our business and our community. We are committed to promoting inclusion for the success of our business.
   

  

 Panasonic is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, and disability, or any other characteristics.

title: Senior Analytics Engineer
description:
Avalanche Studios Group is looking for a Senior Analytics Engineer to join our Data and Insights team. This role will be responsible for creating, designing and maintaining our data architecture. This role requires a deep understanding of data engineering, data modelling, data visualization, event design and best practices in those areas.
   

  

 This is a key role in developing our data lake by bridging the gap between our data engineer and our data scientists as well as ensuring that our data is timely, easy to work with and trusted across the company.
   

  

**What You'll Do**
* Develop and implement processes to extract, clean, and transform data from various sources to ensure it is ready for consumption/analysis.
* Collaborate with data scientists and analysts to understand their data needs and provide them with reliable datasets / data marts.
* Design and implement data models using industry best practices that capture a complete ecosystem view of in- and out-of-game experiences (major game analytics KPI’s, user profile/history) - while ensuring accuracy, compliance, scalability, and long-term usability.
* Create and maintain comprehensive data documentation, including data dictionaries, schemas, and metadata. Ensure that data definitions are consistent and easily accessible to all stakeholders.
* Monitor data pipelines and systems to ensure high availability and performance alongside our data engineer ; implement automated data quality checks, validation processes and alerts ensuring the accuracy and reliability of data.
* Write efficient, maintainable, and scalable code using best practices.
* Conduct code and SQL reviews and provide feedback to ensure high standards of code quality within the team.
* Stay updated with the latest trends, technologies and best practices in data engineering and analytics and share knowledge with the team.


**Who You Are**
* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Data Engineering, or a related field ; with 5+ years experience as an Analytics Engineer, Data Engineer, Data Architect, or similar role.
* Strong proficiency in SQL and experience in designing efficient and scalable data models with large data sets across multiple teams.
* Experience with ETL/ELT processes and tools (e.g. Apache Airflow, Talend, DBT... DBT being a big plus).
* Familiarity with cloud-based data platforms (e.g. AWS, GCP, Azure… GCP being a big plus) ; and experience with “Infrastructure as Code” process (e.g. Terraform).
* Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail ; coupled with strong communication skills and the ability to work collaboratively with cross-functional teams.
* Passion for gaming and a deep understanding of the gaming industry is a plus!


*Note: Research shows under-represented groups in the industry might not apply to a job if they don’t check off every requirement. At Avalanche, we’re more about your drive to learn and interest in tackling challenges than what’s on your resume! So don’t stress if you don’t tick every box— shoot us your application anyway! Diverse opinions and different experiences are what make our teams so great, not itemized lists of qualifications.*
**The Stockholm location**
 Our Stockholm office is situated in Södermalm, the beating heart of the city’s game industry. It’s a physical representation of our games – the lush vegetation of Just Cause and theHunter: Call of the Wild meets corroded metal straight out of Mad Max. It has everything you need to make it your creative base camp.
   

  

**Our values**
 At Avalanche Studios Group, we create worlds beyond limits. But that’s not exclusive to our games, so we’re committed to developing a diverse and inclusive workplace. All Avalanchers have a shared responsibility to create an open work environment where everyone is treated equally and respectfully. Being part of our world is not contingent on your heritage, your gender, or sexual orientation. It’s all about your passion and creativity.
   

  

**How To Apply**
 To apply for this position, please register below and provide your CV in English. We review applications continuously. All further studio-related information is provided under a non-disclosure agreement.
   

  

*At Avalanche Studios Group we're always on the lookout for talented individuals to join our passionate team, but please note that relocation assistance is not available for this role.*

title: Junior Account Executive, Finland (Enterprise)
description:
**About The Role 🚀** 




 As a
 **Junior Account Executive,** 
 you will own the full sales cycle end-to-end for
 **Enterprise customers in Finland** 
 , selling Advania Sweden’s offerings within
 **ServiceNow (including CRM in ServiceNow)** 
 and
 **Operations Technology (OT)** 
 . You will work from a defined strategy and a prioritized account list set by sales leadership—and turn that into qualified pipeline and signed deals.
 



  





 This role is for someone who wants to develop in complex enterprise sales, enjoys structured work, and thrives in a position that connects customer needs with internal expertise. You will build trust with senior stakeholders in Finland, while working closely with our specialists in Sweden to shape the right value story and solution approach.
 



  





**Travel and location** 




 You will travel to Finland to meet customers when required by the deal and the customer stakeholders. At the same time, the role is based at our Stockholm office (Liljeholmen). Being close to our core teams in Sweden is essential for quickly building a deep understanding of our delivery and offering, collaborating effectively with specialists, and maintaining speed and quality throughout the sales process.
 



  





**Your tasks 📝** 




 You will take ownership of a defined set of prioritized Enterprise accounts in Finland and run structured outreach to create qualified opportunities. In customer meetings (in Finnish), you will lead discovery and guide conversations towards clear next steps—building credibility through preparation, curiosity, and precision in how you capture needs and decision criteria.
 



 As opportunities progress, you will coordinate closely with internal specialists to shape value propositions and solution narratives within ServiceNow and OT. You will drive momentum through consistent follow-up, stakeholder mapping, and disciplined qualification, while maintaining high-quality documentation in
 **ServiceNow CRM** 
 so that pipeline and forecasts are reliable.
 



  





**In practice, this means you will:** 



* Drive outreach and meeting creation with prioritized Enterprise accounts in Finland.
* Lead Finnish-language discovery and qualification: map current situation, needs, stakeholders, decision process, timeline and risks.
* Engage internal specialists at the right time and align customer dialogue, solution shaping and proposals into a coherent progression plan.
* Contribute to proposal preparation, handle negotiations within agreed frameworks, and drive deals to signature.
* Work in a structured, data-driven way in ServiceNow CRM: pipeline management, activities, follow-ups, forecasting and reporting.
* Share insights from the Finnish market to continuously improve how we engage and win.



  





**Scope note:** 
 The role covers only
 **ServiceNow (including CRM in ServiceNow)** 
 and
 **Operations Technology (OT)** 
 , and only the
 **Enterprise segment in Finland** 
 .
 



  





**What you bring 🤩** 




 You have enough B2B sales experience to know that complex deals are won through structure, persistence and stakeholder management, not improvisation. You are comfortable engaging new senior contacts, asking sharp questions, and turning early interest into a clear plan: who decides, why it matters, what the timeline looks like, and what risks could derail the deal.
 



 You take pride in being reliable. You prepare, you follow up, and you document properly. You also handle resistance well seeing objections as information you can work with rather than something to avoid. Most importantly, you are motivated to learn our offering deeply and improve week by week through coaching and deliberate practice.
 



  





**We expect you to have:** 



* Fluent Finnish (spoken and written) – all external customer communication is in Finnish.
* A few years of documented B2B sales experience, taking opportunities through multiple stages and closing deals (with support when needed).
* Experience working with complex deals and multiple stakeholders.
* Strong ability to adapt communication to audience and situation and drive conversations towards clear next steps.
* A structured approach to qualification and progression (needs, decision process, timeline, risks) and the discipline to follow through.
* High CRM discipline: documentation quality, pipeline hygiene, follow-up and forecasting (ServiceNow CRM).
* Confidence in objection handling and resilience through longer enterprise sales cycles.



 Nice to have
 


* Experience in software sales (SaaS, platform, licensing or subscription).
* Exposure to ServiceNow (platform/modules) or similar platform services.
* Experience at the IT/OT intersection—industry, operations, production, or high-availability environments.
* Strong English skills for internal collaboration and coaching.



  





**Why Advania 💜** 




 At Advania, we combine high trust with clear ownership. You will work close to experienced specialists and get structured coaching to develop your sales craft in complex enterprise deals. We value a low-ego, collaborative culture where preparation, follow-through and customer impact matter, and where you are encouraged to ask for support early to keep momentum and quality high.
 



  





 We believe it is our people who make the difference. We see diversity as a strength, and we focus on building long-term relationships and solutions that enable sustainable growth, for our customers and for our teams.
 



  





**We look forward to receiving your application! 👋** 




 We encourage you to express your interest as soon as possible, as we review applications on an ongoing basis. We welcome applicants with diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives, as we know that a broad range of diversity enriches us all and strengthens both efficiency and creativity in our work.
 



 As part of our recruitment process, final candidates undergo a mandatory background check, which serves as a qualitative step before making an employment decision. Advania also collaborates with Ljung & Sjöberg to support a proactive and healthy workplace in relation to alcohol and drug use.
 



  





**Please do not hesitate to reach out if you have any questions**

title: Senior Security Officer Physical Security
description:
**Description**
 Welcome to SSC Space (formerly Swedish Space Corporation), the perfect workplace for those passionate about technology in general and space in particular.
   

  

 With space organizations, research institutes, institutional and commercial actors as customers, we offer specialist expertise in satellite communications, space consulting services, rocket and balloon systems, flight test services, satellite propulsion systems, and much more.
   

  

 SSC has one of the world’s largest global networks of ground stations for satellite communications. We own and operate the Esrange Space Center in Kiruna, northern Sweden, with the aim of launching satellites in the near future.
   

  

 Are you fueled by a passion for people and the ambition to create an awesome workplace?
   

  

 Are you ready to embark on an exciting journey with us to help Earth benefit from space?
   

  

 If you answer yes to these questions, we invite you to seize the opportunity to make a significant impact as Senior Security Officer, Physical Security in our Group Security and Resilience team. We are looking for a dedicated person who shares our core values of Customer passion, Care, Collaboration, Curiosity, and Courage.
   

  

 Group Security & Resilience is responsible for Security Management, Resilience Management, Export Control and Quality for all SSC Group.
   

  

 The team has a collaborative workstyle and supports each other to deliver a professional, open and transparent risk-based security & resilience governance throughout the whole value chain.
   

  

**Requirements**
 As Senior Security Officer in Group Security & Resilience you are the expert in Physical Security and top node for all physical security related questions and concerns.
   

  

 Main responsibility for leading and supporting in the Physical Security domain, including but not limited to:
   

  

* Developing and ensuring internal requirements in line with legal and regulatory requirements.
* Drafting, remitting and communicating internal steering and supporting documents.
* Driving global Physical Security forum.
* Follow up implementation in the Business Areas.
* Develop training materials and conduct training sessions.
* Support colleagues in other Security and Resilience domains in scope of the Group Function.


**Responsibilities**
 You bring solid experience within Physical Security and are comfortable taking ownership of the domain in a global context. With a strong understanding of security operations, you are able to combine strategic thinking with hands-on execution when needed.
   

  

 Working in complex environments comes naturally, and you remain calm under pressure. Prioritization, planning and structure are strengths, enabling you to make sound judgments even when conditions change or time is limited.
   

  

 As a colleague, high integrity and reliability are a given. Collaboration comes easily, and you contribute with energy, curiosity and a willingness to explore new ideas, while staying flexible as circumstances evolve.
   

  

 A results driven mindset, combined with strong networking, communication and presentation skills, allows you to build trust and engage effectively with stakeholders across the organization.
   

  

 Fluency in both English and Swedish, verbally and in writing, is a requirement for this role.
   

  

**Benefits**
 WE OFFER YOU
   

  

* A unique opportunity to contribute to a wide range of projects and play an active role in both the Swedish and international space industry.
* An excellent opportunity for personal and career development.
* An exciting, international workplace characterized by an open atmosphere and proud employees all over the world.


**Additional Benefits**
* In addition to vacation and traditional public holidays, we offer extra paid leave throughout the year, such as time off between Christmas and New Year,Ascension Day,amounting to 6-11 extra days depending on the calendar.
* Wellness allowanceof SEK 5,000 per year.
* Healthbenefits such as voluntary health insurance and regular health checks for eligible employees.
* Access to a benefit bike through a tax-efficient leasing program.
* Support for employee-driven activities that promote connection, health, and culture across teams.
* Collective Agreement, includinggood pensionsplans, parentalpayand insurance.


 LOCATION
   

  

 This is a full-time, permanent position based at our office in Solna.
   

  

 FURTHER INFORMATION
   

  

 For questions regarding the position, you are welcome to contact:
   

  

 Hiring Manager Emma Stenholm, Chief Security Officer, +46 76 762 13 22
   

  

 For questions regarding employment terms or union-related matters, you are welcome to contact our union representatives:
   

  

 Sveriges Ingenjörer: SverigesIngenjorer-AF@sscspace.com
   

  

 Unionen: unionen@sscspace.com
   

  

  

  

 NEXT STEP
   

  

 If this opportunity sparks your interest, we encourage you to apply as soon as possible! We review applications continuously and will conduct interviews throughout the process.
   

  

 You can submit your application up until 2026-03-05, which is the latest possible date for consideration.
   

  

 Please note, however, that the position may be filled before this date, and the advertisement may therefore close earlier. This allows us to manage the process efficiently and give each application the attention it deserves.
   

  

 When applying, please include your CV. While you are welcome to include a brief personal note or letter, our evaluation will primarily focus on your skills and qualifications as outlined in your CV, ensuring a fair and unbiased recruitment process.
   

  

 This position is security-sensitive and requires approved security vetting and background check in accordance with the protective security act.
   

  

**Additional Requirements Regarding Citizenship May Apply.**
 WE HELP EARTH BENEFIT FROM SPACE
   

  

 SSC Space is a leading global provider of advanced space services, with more than 50 years of experience. We help space organizations, research institutes, commercial and institutional actors from all over the world to get access to space.
   

  

 With local presence on all continents and about 700 committed employees, we offer specialist expertise in satellite communications and satellite control services, spacecraft operations, rocket and balloon systems, launch services and flight test services, as well as engineering, operations and consultancy services for space missions.
   

  

 We help Earth benefit from Space. Learn more at sscspace.com

title: Product Marketing Manager
description:
**The Position**
 We are looking for a motivated and experienced Product Marketing Manager to join our team. This is a critical role responsible for driving the marketing strategy and execution for our DLC launches and supporting the Go-to-Market (GTM) plans. This role requires a deep understanding of PC and console game marketing, excellent cross-functional collaboration skills, and a passion for our products!
   

  

**What You'll Do**
* Develop and implement comprehensive marketing campaigns to drive awareness, engagement, and sales for each DLC launch.Identify target audiences, key messaging, and marketing channels to maximize campaign effectiveness.
* Manage the campaign budget, ensuring optimal allocation of resources across various initiatives.
* Oversee the quality of marketing assets, including trailers, gameplay videos, social media content, and website updates.
* Collaborate closely with internal teams (PR, Community, Art, Development, Partnership) and manage external agencies to ensure alignment on marketing objectives.
* Establish and track KPIs to measure campaign performance and identify areas for improvement.
* Take on the responsibility as a People Manager.


**Who You Are**
* A strategic thinker with strong project leadership and operational excellence.
* Proven experience orchestrating large, multi-team, multi-channel marketing campaigns.
* Comfortable working with fast-paced timelines and high business impact.
